summary: Full exploration: design every diagnostic-canvas step shape (The Meter), synthesize one data model on the real schema, spec the visual vocabulary, adversarially prove no air gaps, build the updated prototype.

perShape.shapeName: Electrical Circuit / Path (PARENT shape ‚Äî the shared electrical canvas)
perShape.triggeredBy: This PARENT shape is selected automatically whenever the leading test_action for the focused component has observationMethod='electrical_measurement_at_pin' OR 'waveform_capture' ‚Äî the two methods that put a meter/scope lead on a component_pin. (observationMethod is the shape selector, real enum at schema.ts:873-884.) The SUB-shape is then chosen by the electrical sub-quantity, which is NOT a column today ‚Äî it is read from meterMode (text, schema.ts:886) when present: 'volts-dc'/'V'‚Üívolts, 'volts-drop'‚Üídrop, 'ohms'/'Œ©'‚Üíohms, 'duty'/'%'/'Hz'‚Üíduty, 'amps'/'A'‚Üíamps; observationMethod='waveform_capture' always‚Üíwaveform regardless of meterMode. Until meterMode is populated, the sub-quantity falls back to the pin's roleAbbreviation (component_pins.roleAbbreviation, real, e.g. '12V'‚Üívolts, 'GND'‚Üíohms/continuity, 'PWM'‚Üíduty, 'SIG'‚Üívolts or waveform). Canonical trigger in this scene: Lift Pump ¬∑ 12V Power pin (slug lp-12v, id 6d88ec94, roleAbbreviation '12V') ‚Üí volts-DC sub-shape.
perShape.screenContents: Almost the entire screen is the diagram, but only the tested circuit is lit: source ‚Üí part ‚Üí ground drawn as a directed path. For the canonical step the lit elements are exactly five ‚Äî Lift Pump Relay (source/switch) ‚Üí 12V wire ‚Üí Electric Lift Pump (the part under test, full ink) ‚Üí ground wire ‚Üí Chassis Ground ‚Äî plus the FRP Sensor held faint in the corner as the single 'why we're here' anchor (it is the symptom signal). The other ~19 fuel components stay drawn but receded. The hero is the READING in the bottom Meter band: a large mono tabular EXPECT plate (12 V) beside the live NOW value for the current scenario ('12V briefly during prime, then 0V') and one verdict word. A hairline navy leader runs from the tapped pin up to the Meter so the diagram is provably WHERE the number comes from. The probe-hookup overlay is drawn on the circuit itself: for volts-DC, one lead-dot sits on the lit 12V pin and a faint reference lead-dot on the ground symbol (one-lead-to-ground hookup). Below the EXPECT/NOW row, dimmed one level: connector / wire-count / location / one-line Operational Theori (from component.role). The failure tree (pin.missingLogic) is COLLAPSED. Provenance is a quiet mono caption under the value: 'from theory ¬∑ why?'.
perShape.lightVocabDrawing: No rectangles, no cards. Each element is a small role-colored point (9px node marker) + a one-line Instrument-Serif name; kind/location are suppressed on the canvas (they live in the Meter). The directed path is clean elbowed lines, each colored by electrical role via the six --role-* tokens (which must be DEFINED ‚Äî see gaps): --role-12v warm-red (power=hot), --role-ground graphite, --role-signal navy, --role-5v-ref/--role-low-ref cool, --role-pwm violet. The active wire carries the existing .wire.is-active treatment (3.5px + drop-shadow glow) and a dash-flow animation that means current; receded wires drop to --vt-recede (0.16) and freeze. The active pin is a navy-haloed dot (the eye lands here). THE METER-HOOKUP OVERLAY is the shared mechanic that the sub-shapes vary ‚Äî it is drawn on the canvas where the leads actually go: volts-DC = one bright lead-dot on the pin + a faint reference lead-dot on the ground node; voltage-DROP = TWO bright lead-dots, one each end of the SAME wire segment, with a bracket spanning it (measuring across the conductor, not to ground); ohms/continuity = two lead-dots with the source node de-energized (wire drawn cold, no flow-dash ‚Äî 'key off' look) and a small Œ© glyph at the clamp; duty-PWM = lead-dot on the signal pin + the wire rendered as a square-wave dash instead of steady flow; amps = a clamp-ring glyph drawn AROUND the wire (not a dot on the pin) ‚Äî the only inductive, non-contact hookup; waveform = a probe-dot on the pin plus a scope-window frame docked in the Meter. The GAUGE in the Meter band also varies: volts/drop/ohms = a value-against-band plate (EXPECT number + tolerance arc); duty = a 0‚Äì100% ring with the live duty sweeping; amps = same value plate with A unit; waveform = a live trace against a fixed known-good reference trace overlay (the only sub-shape whose hero is a curve, not a digit). The EXPECT FORMAT string is composed from expectedValue + expectedUnit + ¬± expectedTolerance.
perShape.meterBlock: Bottom Meter band (~30% desktop, detented sheet mobile), led by the reading. For the canonical volts-DC case: EXPECT plate shows '12 V' (composed expectedValue 12 + expectedUnit 'V'); a tolerance line '¬±1.5 V' when expectedTolerance is present; NOW column self-labels the scenario ('NOW ¬∑ Key On ¬∑ Engine Off') and shows the live prose reading '12V briefly during prime, then 0V'; verdict word derived from branch_logic.verdict (ok/warn/fail) ‚Üí 'in range' / 'check' / 'out of range'. HOW SUB-SHAPES VARY THE BLOCK: volts/drop/ohms/amps ‚Üí EXPECT/NOW/verdict value-vs-band (same component, only unit + tolerance + hookup label differ); duty-PWM ‚Üí EXPECT shown as a duty range ('20‚Äì80% ¬∑ varies with load', from expectedReading prose since duty has no single target) with a sweeping ring instead of a fixed digit; waveform ‚Üí the block becomes a TRACE WINDOW (live vs fixed known-good reference, PicoScope convention) with no single EXPECT digit. Verdict suppressed and missingLogic stays collapsed on an in-range reading; on out-of-range the verdict goes red and pin.missingLogic unfolds in place ('0V when commanded ‚Üí relay, fuse, pump driver'). For a LOCATE step the whole EXPECT/NOW block is SUPPRESSED (leads with pin.probeLocation only); for ORIENT no pin is elevated and no reading shows.

perShape.schemaMapping: PARENT-shape select: test_actions.observationMethod ‚àà {electrical_measurement_at_pin, waveform_capture}. SUB-shape select: test_actions.meterMode (currently dropped by loader) with fallback to component_pins.roleAbbreviation. EXPECT format: test_actions.expectedValue + expectedUnit + ('¬±'+expectedTolerance) ‚Äî all four currently dropped by the loader SELECT (load-system-topology.ts:296-313); surfacing them is a loader-SELECT delta only, NOT a new model. The directed path: component_connections rows whose electricalRole is non-null (12v/ground/signal/pwm/5v-ref/low-ref) draw + color the wires; for the canonical case the lp 12V feed is connection ecac52dd (electricalRole '12v', relay‚Üípump) and the ground edge. The lit part + pin: components (Electric Lift Pump, dc4f85b9) + component_pins (lp-12v 6d88ec94 roleAbbreviation '12V' edge 'top'; lp-gnd e572e1c0 roleAbbreviation 'GND' edge 'bottom'). probeLocation/expectedReading/missingLogic/labelGap/sourceProvenance all from component_pins. NOW value: scenario.pinReadings[6d88ec94] = pin_scenario_readings keyed (pinId,scenarioId) ‚Äî at key-on '12V briefly during prime, then 0V'. Scenario label/keyPosition/engineState: system_scenarios (key-on row). Verdict + fork: branch_logic.verdict + routesToTestActionId (routesToTestActionId dropped today). meterMode also tells the gauge which form (ring for duty, trace for waveform). The amber 'needs field check' register pulls from component_pins.labelGap ('wire color, connector pin number, prime duration... not yet captured') and components.electricalContract ('voltage, current draw... not yet captured' on dc4f85b9) ‚Äî surfaced only in the expanded see-source, never on canvas.
gaps: The six --role-* wire tokens (--role-signal, --role-5v-ref, --role-low-ref, --role-pwm, --role-12v, --role-ground) are referenced across topology.css but DEFINED NOWHERE in the app ‚Äî every wire currently renders colorless. PROPOSE: define them in globals.css :root on the element ramp (one-line CSS fix, no DB change). Same for --vt-recede (promote the 0.16 already baked into .wire.off) and --vt-amber-600 (topology.css references it; globals.css defines only --vt-amber-200..500).

The four structured EXPECT columns exist on test_actions but the loader SELECT drops them. PROPOSE: add meterMode, expectedValue, expectedUnit, expectedTolerance to the test_actions select in load-system-topology.ts (lines 296-313) and to TopologyTestAction. This is the single highest-leverage change ‚Äî it turns the prose-only Meter into a real EXPECT-vs-band gauge. NO new model.

NO column for the electrical SUB-quantity (volts vs drop vs ohms vs duty vs Hz vs amps). PROPOSE: do NOT add one ‚Äî derive from meterMode text + roleAbbreviation. Only if curators need it explicit, add a small enum, but prefer derivation to honor 'minimal new fields'.

NO column for hookup form (one-lead / across-two / clamp / scope). PROPOSE: derive from meterMode ('volts-drop'‚Üíacross, 'amps'‚Üíclamp) + observationMethod ('waveform_capture'‚Üíscope); no new column.
branch_logic.routesToTestActionId (the fork target, schema.ts:926) is dropped by the loader, so the BRANCH step cannot light the next part it routes to. PROPOSE: surface it in TopologyBranch.

NO reliable per-(pin,scenario) in-range boolean. pin_scenario_readings.reading is free prose ('12V briefly during prime, then 0V'); the only structured fault signal is scenario.kind (per whole-diagram, not per-pin). PROPOSE: either add inRange:boolean to pin_scenario_readings, or author branch_logic.condition to evaluate expectedValue¬±expectedTolerance ‚Äî otherwise red + failure-tree-unfold degrade to coarse scenario.kind detection.

NO waveform-reference column for the scope sub-shape's fixed known-good trace. PROPOSE (deferred): a small reference-waveform asset/points field on test_actions when waveform_capture is authored; until then waveform falls back to the value-plate + expectedObservation prose.
perShape.incompleteState: Honest, never fabricated. (1) If expectedValue/expectedUnit are absent (loader not yet surfacing them, or curator left them null), the EXPECT plate does NOT invent a number ‚Äî it shows the prose pin.expectedReading ('12V when commanded by PCM') in the plate slot and the gauge renders without a numeric band; the verdict is suppressed (neutral), not guessed. (2) If meterMode is null, the sub-shape falls back to roleAbbreviation; if that is also ambiguous, default to the volts value-plate (the safest generic electrical readout) and draw the one-lead-to-ground hookup. (3) If there is no per-(pin,scenario) in-range flag, the verdict stays NEUTRAL (no red, no green) and the failure tree stays COLLAPSED ‚Äî we never color a reading wrong we cannot prove wrong. (4) Provenance is always shown: all 9 pins here are TRAINING-CONFIRMED ‚Üí 'from theory' in neutral graphite (the default, not an alarm). (5) The amber 'needs field check' register appears ONLY from real labelGap / electricalContract facts ('wire color, connector pin number, prime duration ‚Äî not yet captured') and ONLY inside the expanded see-source path ‚Äî never as a standing badge on the node. The screen states what it knows and stays silent on what it does not; 'needs field check' honesty.
perShape.mobile: 375‚Äì414px hard gate. 44px top ribbon: vehicle + code + one scenario chip ('Key On ‚ñæ'); 'whole system' and 'curator' controls drop. The lit circuit auto-frames into the TOP ~45‚Äì48% and fills the width; the source‚Üípart‚Üíground path stacks vertically (relay on top ‚Üí pump center ‚Üí chassis below) so it reads top-to-bottom on a narrow column. Pin dots are ‚â•48px touch targets. The Meter is a non-modal bottom sheet with peek/half/full detents (NO scrim at peek/half; canvas stays live above). The reading is the largest type on the phone: EXPECT plate ~38px mono tnum + NOW value below; verdict chip wraps under. Provenance 'from theory ¬∑ why?' is a ‚â•44px tap target that expands within the half detent. Secondary facts (connector/wires/Theori) sit below the fold, drag-up to reveal. The hookup-overlay still draws on the circuit above the sheet. CRITICAL coupling: as the sheet rises the camera pans the lit pump UP so the sheet NEVER covers the part under test (Turo technique) ‚Äî tested specifically at 375px as an acceptance gate. A thin 'Now ¬∑ Key On' status line sits just above the sheet (the only survivor of the desktop scenario strip). For the duty sub-shape the ring scales down but stays the hero; for waveform the trace window takes the full sheet width at the half detent.
perShape.sceneExample: Rendered from THIS 6.7L P0087 scene, canonical volts-DC instance: the tech is under a 2018 F-250, key on, back-probing the lift pump power wire. observationMethod='electrical_measurement_at_pin' on the lp-12v test_action selects the Circuit/Path parent; meterMode (once surfaced) reads volts ‚Üí volts-DC sub-shape (today derived from roleAbbreviation '12V'). The canvas lights Lift Pump Relay ‚Üí [--role-12v warm-red wire, flowing] ‚Üí Electric Lift Pump (full ink, navy-haloed 12V pin) ‚Üí [--role-ground graphite wire] ‚Üí Chassis Ground, with FRP Sensor faint in the corner ('why we're here'). Hookup overlay: bright lead-dot on the 12V pin, faint reference lead-dot on the ground symbol. The Meter rises: EXPECT plate '12 V' (from expectedValue 12 + expectedUnit 'V'; would also show '¬±1.5 V' if expectedTolerance authored), NOW ¬∑ Key On ¬∑ Engine Off = '12V briefly during prime, then 0V' (pin_scenario_readings, key-on), verdict 'in range', caption 'from theory ¬∑ why?'. Flip the chip to Engine Idle and the NOW digit settles to '12V steady' (idle reading) with the EXPECT band fixed. Sub-shape contrast in the same scene: tapping the lp-gnd pin (roleAbbreviation 'GND') swaps to the ohms/continuity sub-shape ‚Äî wire drawn cold (no flow-dash), Œ© glyph at the clamp, EXPECT '< 0.5 Œ©' (from pin.expectedReading prose until expectedValue authored); the FRP Regulator Pin A (roleAbbreviation 'PWM') would render the duty sub-shape ‚Äî square-wave wire + a 0‚Äì100% ring with 'duty varies with load' (from expected_reading), no fixed EXPECT digit.

perShape.shapeName: The Meter ‚Äî Voltage DC (one-lead-to-point + ground reference)
perShape.triggeredBy: Code picks this shape automatically from test_actions.observationMethod === 'electrical_measurement_at_pin' (the shape SELECTOR), then narrows to the Voltage-DC sub-shape via the electrical sub-quantity. There is no sub-quantity column today, so the deterministic fallback is meterMode: when meterMode === 'volts-dc' (or 'V'/'VDC') AND expectedUnit === 'V' ‚Üí Voltage-DC. A secondary signal confirming "one lead to a point + ground reference" (vs across-two / clamp / scope) is that the implicated component_pin has roleAbbreviation '12V' (a power/supply pin) wired by a component_connection whose electricalRole === '12v', with a sibling pin roleAbbreviation 'GND' / electricalRole === 'ground' available as the reference. For the canonical scene the trigger resolves on pin lp-12v (role '12V', expectedReading '12V when commanded by PCM') fed by the electrical-wire connection role '12v' ("Relay contact supplies switched battery voltage to the electric lift pump motor"). When meterMode is null (the data-gap case), fall back to: observationMethod electrical_measurement_at_pin + expectedUnit 'V' + a non-ground role pin ‚áí Voltage-DC; if expectedUnit is also null, degrade to the generic electrical shape with a neutral verdict.
perShape.screenContents: HERO is the reading, not the truck. Top: a thin recessed mono ribbon ‚Äî "2018 Ford F-250 ¬∑ 6.7L Power Stroke ¬∑ P0087 rail pressure low" with a recessed scenario chip "Key On ¬∑ Engine Off ‚ñæ" at the right (no persistent scenario bar). Canvas (the upper ~70%): only the lift-pump corner is lit ‚Äî the Lift Pump Relay (focus), a single live 12V wire dropping into the Electric Lift Pump (active, navy marker), the pump's GND leader running down to Chassis Ground (focus, but quiet), and far off to the side the FRP Sensor drawn faintly as the one "why we're here" anchor. The other ~21 fuel-system parts stay drawn but receded to 0.16 ‚Äî present, not gone. The tapped 12V pin carries a navy halo and a hairline dashed leader rising to the Meter. Bottom band (~30%, "THE METER") leads with the instrument block for THIS pin in THIS scenario: a label row "LIFT PUMP ¬∑ 12V POWER" + a small mono "PROBE" tag; the probe-where line "back-probe the power wire at the lift pump connector under the truck near the tank"; then EXPECT 12V (fat tabular mono, in a quiet expected-band frame) | NOW ¬∑ Key On ¬∑ Engine Off "12V briefly during prime, then 0V" | a single verdict chip "‚óê in range". Under the NOW value, a quiet graphite caption "from theory ¬∑ why?". Below, one dimmed secondary line "connector ¬∑ 2 wires ¬∑ ground ¬∑ Operational Theory" (one tap down). Dimmed/below-fold: the secondary facts grid (wire_summary, this-pin role, the amber "needs field check" label_gap, the serif Operational Theory from component.body), and the failure tree (missing_logic) which stays COLLAPSED on this in-range reading.
perShape.lightVocabDrawing: No rectangles, no cards. Marks: each part is a small role-colored point + a one-line Instrument-Serif name; type/location never printed on the canvas. The active part (Electric Lift Pump) gets a navy-ringed marker (--vt-signal-500 halo) and italic serif name. DIRECTED LINES with meaning: a single 12V supply wire drawn warm-red (--role-12v, "power = hot") from the Lift Pump Relay DOWN into the pump's 12V pin, carrying a slow dash-flow (stroke-dasharray 5 6, flow keyframe) to read as live current ‚Äî flow animates ONLY because this is a PROBE step on a steady-12v wire; the GND return runs graphite (--role-ground) DOWN from the pump to Chassis Ground, no flow (it's the reference, quiet). All other harness wires are .wire.off (opacity 0.16, animation:none) ‚Äî hidden-not-deleted, returning only in whole-system. METER-HOOKUP OVERLAY: this is one-lead-to-a-point + ground reference, so the overlay draws the red meter lead clipping onto the lit 12V pin (a small probe glyph at the pin, navy) and a ground-clip glyph at the Chassis Ground point (or the battery-negative reference) ‚Äî the two leads of a DC voltage measurement drawn exactly where they physically go, which doubles as the WHERE proof. GAUGE/TRACE: the gauge is the EXPECT band itself ‚Äî a fixed reference plate reading 12 V with the live NOW value set beside it (PicoScope "reference stays fixed, compare by perception" convention); no oscilloscope trace (that belongs to the waveform shape). A hairline dashed leader connects the lit pin up to the Meter card so the number is visibly anchored to its source.
perShape.meterBlock: EXPECT / NOW / verdict (the full instrument block ‚Äî this is the canonical "compare a value to a band" case). EXPECT: the band reference, fat mono tabular "12 V" rendered from test_actions.expectedValue (12) + expectedUnit ('V'), with expectedTolerance shown as the band tolerance when present; sub-note from expectedObservation / pin.expectedReading ("12V when commanded by PCM ‚Äî key-on prime, cranking, or running"). NOW: self-labeled "NOW ¬∑ Key On ¬∑ Engine Off" (scenario folded into the column header, not a separate bar) showing the live pin_scenario_readings text "12V briefly during prime, then 0V"; on scenario flip to Idle it settles (damped tnum, ~160ms) to "12V steady", EXPECT stays fixed. VERDICT: a single chip "‚óê in range" ‚Äî driven by a range comparison of NOW vs EXPECT¬±tolerance. Under NOW: provenance caption "from theory ¬∑ why?" (graphite ‚Äî the default for TRAINING-CONFIRMED, not an alarm). The failure tree from pin.missing_logic ("0V when commanded ‚Üí relay, fuse, pump driver; 12V but pump silent ‚Üí ground or motor") stays COLLAPSED on in-range and unfolds in place ONLY when NOW falls out of range (then the verdict chip + leg color go red).

perShape.screenContents: Hero is the verdict, not a fat number: a binary CONTINUITY / OPEN readout because a ground check is pass/open, not a value-on-a-band. The lit circuit is just two points and the wire between them ‚Äî the Lift Pump GND point and the Chassis Ground stud ‚Äî drawn at full ink with everything else (tank, filter, CP4, rails, 8 injectors, PCM, FRP) receded to the 0.16 floor. No 12V relay leg is lit; this step does not involve the power side. Below the circuit, the bottom Meter (risen on tap) leads with WHERE: "Resistance check from the ground wire at the lift pump connector to chassis ground" (pin.probeLocation verbatim), then the instrument block EXPECT "< 0.5 Œ©" against NOW, then the verdict chip. Dimmed below the fold: connector / 2 wires / what each does / location / Operational Theori, and the amber "needs field check" fact (wire color + exact chassis stud location). The failure tree (pin.missingLogic: "Open or high resistance ‚Üí broken ground wire... OR corroded ground point") stays COLLAPSED unless NOW is out of range. Provenance caption under the value reads "from theory ¬∑ why?" in neutral graphite (the pin is TRAINING-CONFIRMED). The scenario chip in the ribbon is recessed and DE-EMPHASIZED for this shape because continuity is key-state-independent (see meterBlock).
perShape.lightVocabDrawing: Two role-colored points joined by ONE ground-role line ‚Äî this is the only electrical sub-shape drawn as a span between two equal endpoints rather than a single tapped pin with current flowing toward it. Mark A: the Lift Pump GND point (small graphite point, --role-ground = --vt-fg-2, the pin edge is "bottom" so it sits at the underside of the pump mark). Mark B: the Chassis Ground stud (a distinct ground-symbol point, the three-bar earth glyph, not another pin chip ‚Äî it is a destination, not a component). The connecting wire uses --role-ground and is drawn STATIC with NO dash-flow animation: there is no current direction in a key-off resistance check, so animating it would lie. Meter-hookup overlay: TWO probe leads, not one ‚Äî a lead glyph clamped at A and a second lead glyph at B, with a faint hairline bracket spanning A‚ÜîB labeled with the omega symbol, making it unmistakable this is a measurement BETWEEN two points (the voltage shape, by contrast, shows one lead on the pin and the other to a ground reference off-screen). The active span gets the navy focus halo on both endpoints simultaneously (both ends are "the thing under test"). A hairline leader rises from the midpoint of the A‚ÜîB span up to the Meter's verdict, since the verdict belongs to the span, not to one pin. No rectangles, no card around the pump; the pump is its serif name + its two edge points only.

Verdict: a binary CONTINUITY (good, navy tick) vs OPEN / HIGH-Œ© (red) chip ‚Äî NOT an "in range / low / high" three-way like the voltage shape, because a ground is pass/fail. Critically, the NOW column's scenario label is SUPPRESSED-to-neutral here: instead of "NOW ¬∑ Key On ¬∑ Engine Off" it reads "NOW ¬∑ key-off (any key state)" because the GND continuity reading is identical across all 8 scenarios in the real data (verified: 7 of 8 say exactly "Continuity to chassis", idle adds "¬∑ sinking pump current"). perShape.shapeName: Voltage Drop Across a Load (Under Load)
perShape.triggeredBy: observationMethod = `electrical_measurement_at_pin` (the real enum value, the shape selector) PLUS the electrical sub-quantity = voltage-drop AND a two-point/across-a-load hookup. NEITHER the sub-quantity nor the hookup exists as a column today, so on current data this test_action is INDISTINGUISHABLE from a single-point DC-voltage or a resistance test ‚Äî all three are `electrical_measurement_at_pin`. To pick THIS shape automatically the code must read: observationMethod=`electrical_measurement_at_pin` + meterMode=`vdrop` (proposed enum value in the existing-but-dropped `meter_mode` column) + scenarioRequired in the load set (`idle`/`medium-load`/`heavy-load` ‚Äî voltage drop is meaningless key-off). The under-load gate is real and already expressible: scenarioRequired=`heavy-load` is the canonical trigger. Lead-of-leads = observationMethod; meterMode disambiguates the three electrical sub-shapes; scenarioRequired proves "under load."
perShape.screenContents: HERO: the EXPECT/NOW/verdict instrument block in The Meter, reading a DROP not an absolute ‚Äî EXPECT renders "< 0.1 V" (small-is-good, the master-tech inversion: here a near-zero number is PASS, a big number is the fault), NOW renders the live drop for the load scenario, verdict chip = good/high. The canvas shows ONLY the load and its two probe ends: the Electric Lift Pump motor drawn as the load element in full ink, its 12V feed coming in from the Lift Pump Relay and its ground leaving to the Chassis Ground stud ‚Äî these two spans ARE the circuit under test. The FRP sensor sits at 0.45 as the quiet "why we're here" anchor; the other 22 parts recede to 0.16. Two probe marks, not one: a black-lead mark and a red-lead mark planted at the two ends of the span being dropped (across the ground side: pump ground pin -> chassis stud; OR across the feed: relay output -> pump 12V pin), with the meter bridging them ‚Äî this is the one shape where the meter visibly hooks ACROSS two points, not one point to ground. Below the fold (one tap): which two points, what wire, the load's current draw context, Operational Theori, and (only if NOW is high) the missingLogic failure tree. NOT shown: absolute 12V value (that's the sibling DC-voltage shape), any non-load scenario reading, paragraphs on canvas.
perShape.lightVocabDrawing: No rectangles. The LOAD (lift pump motor) is the one full-ink mark: a small role-neutral node point with its serif name "Electric Lift Pump" ‚Äî but rendered with the universal load glyph (a fine resistor-zigzag / coil stroke inline on the wire passing through it) so the eye reads "current is doing work here," because a voltage-drop test is defined by the load between the probes. TWO directed lines meet at the load: the 12V feed line in from the relay (warm-red `--role-12v`, current-flow dash animating toward the pump because the scenario is under-load and current is real), and the ground return line out to the chassis stud (graphite `--role-ground`). THE METER-HOOKUP OVERLAY is the distinguishing geometry: two probe leads drawn AS A SPAN ‚Äî a red-lead tick at one end of the measured segment and a black-lead tick at the other, joined by a faint navy bridge arc that lifts off the canvas down into The Meter (the hairline leader), so it visibly reads "meter is connected ACROSS these two, measuring the difference," never "one lead to a pin, one to ground." The two probe ticks sit on the REAL wire ends (relay-out + pump-12V for a feed drop, or pump-GND + chassis-stud for a ground drop). The GAUGE in The Meter is a short linear scale anchored at 0 with the tolerance band hugging zero (0 -> 0.1V green hairband, then widening graphite-to-red as the drop grows) and a damped tabular-numeral needle ‚Äî the inverted gauge where the needle resting at the LEFT (near zero) is good. No dotted grid; OKLCH bond-paper ground only.
perShape.meterBlock: EXPECT / NOW / verdict format, with the small-is-good inversion made explicit. EXPECT plate: "< 0.1 V" (mono tabular, with the "<" rendered as part of the value, sourced from expectedValue=0.1 + expectedUnit="V" + a proposed comparator). Sub-note under EXPECT: "drop across the load under load ‚Äî near-zero is good." NOW column: "NOW ¬∑ Heavy Load" with the live drop value settling like a damped needle; if no measured value yet, NOW shows the from-theory expected drop, not a fabricated reading. Verdict chip: good (drop within band, navy "‚óê in spec") OR high (drop exceeds band, red "‚ñ≤ voltage drop high ‚Üí resistance in this leg"). Provenance caption beneath NOW: "from theory ¬∑ why?" (graphite, TRAINING-CONFIRMED). The verdict here is a COMPARE-against-band (the gauge band hugs zero), not a good-vs-bad two-trace and not a waveform window ‚Äî so: EXPECT/NOW/verdict, gauge-style, with the band as the hero reference.

perShape.schemaMapping: observationMethod=`electrical_measurement_at_pin` selects the electrical family. meter_mode (already on test_actions, just un-dropped from the loader SELECT) = 'vdrop' selects THIS sub-shape vs DC-voltage vs resistance. expected_value/expected_unit/expected_tolerance (the other three dropped columns) populate EXPECT="< 0.1 V" and the gauge band ‚Äî surfacing all four is the single loader-SELECT delta the brief favors over new models. scenario_required='heavy-load' gates NOW to a load scenario and is folded into the NOW column label ("NOW ¬∑ Heavy Load") + the recessed ribbon chip. The two-point span is the genuine gap: propose test_actions.from_pin_id + to_pin_id (two nullable uuid -> component_pins.id) so the overlay plants leads on real pin/wire ends; the measured segment maps to an existing component_connections row (electrical_role 'ground' or '12v' => animated wire edge already supported). missing_logic (component_pins) + branch_logic (condition/verdict/nextAction, plus routesToTestActionId which is currently dropped) drive the failure tree + the next lit part. source_provenance + PROVENANCE_LABEL drive the caption. scenario_wire_states/pin_scenario_readings are per-single-pin and do NOT model a span ‚Äî they animate the wires but cannot store the drop value; tech_outcomes.measured_value/measured_unit/verdict is the right structured home for NOW but is unloaded. NO TopologyTestAction field carries volts/ohms/drop, hookup, or the two-pin span today ‚Äî TopologyTestAction would gain meterMode, expectedValue, expectedUnit, expectedTolerance, comparator, fromPinId, toPinId.
gaps: expected_comparator (lt/lte/eq/gte/gt) ‚Äî MISSING. Voltage-drop is the canonical less-than test; without a comparator the gauge cannot render 'near-zero is GOOD' vs a centered target, and the verdict cannot flip correctly. Propose one nullable text column on test_actions defaulting to 'eq' so no existing shape changes.

The two-point span ‚Äî MISSING and structural. A test_action has one componentId and pins hang singly off components; nothing expresses 'measure ACROSS pump-GND to chassis stud.' Propose test_actions.from_pin_id + to_pin_id (two nullable uuid FKs). This is the load-bearing gap that makes voltage-drop a distinct shape rather than a relabeled single-point read.

The four structured EXPECT columns (meter_mode, expected_value, expected_unit, expected_tolerance) EXIST on test_actions but are DROPPED by the loader SELECT (load-system-topology.ts:298-305). Not missing from the model ‚Äî missing from the wire. Un-dropping them is the cheap win.

A structured measured DROP value for NOW + verdict ‚Äî MISSING from the load path. pin_scenario_readings.reading is free prose AND single-pin (cannot key a span); tech_outcomes.measured_value exists but is not loaded by the topology loader and not in Drizzle defs. Until a structured span measurement exists, NOW honestly shows the from-theory expected drop, not a parsed number.
branch_logic.routesToTestActionId (the fork target that lights the next part when the drop is high) is DROPPED by the loader SELECT ‚Äî needed for the failure-tree 'next part' behavior.
perShape.incompleteState: The drop test cannot fabricate a NOW. When no structured measured drop exists for this span+scenario (the current state ‚Äî there is no span measurement table on the load path), The Meter shows EXPECT "< 0.1 V" fully, and the NOW column reads "not yet measured" in graphite with the gauge needle absent (the band is drawn, the needle is not) ‚Äî never a faked near-zero. The verdict chip is suppressed (no good/high claim without a reading). The two probe-lead marks still render on the real wire ends from probe_location, so the screen still teaches WHERE to hook across ‚Äî the value is honestly blank, the hookup is honestly known. If meter_mode/expected_value are absent (test_action authored before the columns were filled), the shape degrades to the generic electrical single-point read rather than guessing a drop. If from_pin_id/to_pin_id are absent, the across-two-points overlay falls back to one probe mark + the description string, and a quiet "second probe point not yet captured" note sits in the see-source path (amber needs-field-check register, sourced from the real label_gap pattern), never on the canvas.
perShape.mobile: 375-414px: the load (lift pump) auto-frames into the TOP ~45% filling the width, with BOTH probe-lead marks (red + black) visible on the two wire ends ‚Äî the span must read on the phone, so the framing fits the whole measured segment (relay-out -> pump, or pump -> chassis stud) not just one node; pin/probe targets >=48px. The Meter is a non-modal PEEK sheet (~150px, no scrim, canvas stays live above; canvas pans up as the sheet rises so the load is never covered ‚Äî the spine-forbidden failure). Sheet leads with the EXPECT plate "< 0.1 V" as the largest type (mono tnum), NOW beside it ("not yet measured" or the live drop), verdict chip on its own row, then "from theory ¬∑ why?" as a >=44px tap target. perShape.sceneExample: 2018 F-250 6.7L, P0087. After confirming the lift pump gets 12V on command and the ground has continuity at rest, the master-tech step is the differentiator: voltage drop across the ground side UNDER LOAD. The load = Electric Lift Pump motor (components dc4f85b9, kind=pump, electrical_contract notes current draw 'not yet captured'). The span = pump Ground pin (lp-gnd, id e572e1c0, probe_location "Resistance check from the ground wire at the lift pump connector to chassis ground") to the chassis ground stud, measured while the pump is running (scenario_required='heavy-load' / 'idle' with the pump commanded on). Canvas: pump in full ink with the load-coil glyph on the wire through it; warm-red 12V feed in from Lift Pump Relay (04c878de) with current-flow dash; graphite ground out to the chassis stud; FRP sensor (ea8ae2ff) faint at 0.45 as "why we're here." Red-lead tick on the pump ground pin, black-lead tick on the chassis stud, navy bridge arc lifting into The Meter. The Meter: EXPECT "< 0.1 V" (proposed expected_value=0.1, expected_unit='V', meter_mode='vdrop'), sub-note "drop across the ground leg under load ‚Äî near-zero is good," NOW "not yet measured" (no structured span reading exists; honest blank), verdict suppressed, "from theory ¬∑ why?" beneath (TRAINING-CONFIRMED). One tap down: "needs field check ¬∑ wire color, exact chassis stud location ‚Äî not yet captured" (the real lp-gnd label_gap, amber). If a tech later enters 0.45 V, the gauge needle swings right past the band, verdict flips to red "‚ñ≤ voltage drop high ‚Üí resistance in this ground leg," and missing_logic unfolds: "Open or high resistance ‚Üí broken ground wire between the pump and the chassis stud, OR corroded ground point" (the real lp-gnd missing_logic), lighting the chassis stud + connector as the next thing to inspect.

perShape.shapeName: The Meter ‚Äî Duty Cycle / PWM (one lead, engine running, EXPECT a duty%)
perShape.triggeredBy: Code picks this shape automatically when test_actions.observationMethod === 'waveform_capture' AND the implicated pin's component_pins.roleAbbreviation === 'PWM' (all four PWM pins in this scene ‚Äî vcv-a, vcv-b, reg-a, reg-b ‚Äî carry roleAbbreviation:'PWM') AND the PCM‚Üícomponent connection has component_connections.electricalRole === 'pwm'. The duty-vs-(volts/Hz/amps) discrimination is the ONE thing not in the data today: there is no electrical sub-quantity column. So the real trigger today is the combination above; observationMethod='waveform_capture' + roleAbbreviation='PWM' is the deterministic pick. (Distinct from electrical/voltage-DC where roleAbbreviation is '12V'/'5V'/'SIG'/'GND' and observationMethod is 'electrical_measurement_at_pin'.)
perShape.screenContents: HERO: the bottom Meter, led by a small duty TRACE (not a single fat figure) because the truth of PWM is "a square wave is alive and its duty rides with load," not one frozen number. Top band of the Meter: label "FRP REGULATOR ¬∑ PWM (PIN A)" + step tag "PROBE". Probe lead-line: probeLocation prose "Back-probe pin A at the FRP regulator connector (back of DS rail)" prefixed by mono "where". The instrument block, three columns: EXPECT = the duty BAND drawn as a low‚Üíhigh ramp track with the load-appropriate window lit (idle ‚âà moderate, heavy ‚âà near-max) ‚Äî NOT a hard "12V"-style plate, because duty has no single right number, it has a right RANGE for the current load; NOW = the live trace + the real prose reading for THIS (pin,scenario) e.g. "PWM ¬∑ near max duty" with the scenario folded in as "NOW ¬∑ Heavy Load"; VERDICT = a quiet "alive ¬∑ tracking" chip (PWM present and duty moving the right direction with load) rather than in-range/out-of-range, since the diagnostic is "is it modulating," not "is it 47%." Provenance caption under NOW: "from theory ¬∑ why?" (graphite ‚Äî TRAINING-CONFIRMED, the default). CANVAS above (the quiet proof of WHERE): only the PWM corner is lit ‚Äî the PCM as the commanding brain, one directed violet PWM line dropping to the FRP Regulator actuator with its two PWM pins, and the FRP Sensor glowing faintly in the corner as the "why we're here" anchor (the rail-pressure symptom that makes this regulator worth probing). The other ~22 parts stay drawn but receded. Below the fold in the Meter (one tap down): connector ¬∑ 2 wires both to PCM ¬∑ the complementary pin B ¬∑ Operational Theori. The amber "needs field check" register (PWM frequency, duty-cycle range, coil resistance, wire color/pin number ‚Äî not yet captured) lives ONLY in the expanded see-source path, never on the canvas.
perShape.lightVocabDrawing: MARKS: PCM = a graphite role point + serif "PCM" (the commanding node, drawn at anchor weight since it is the source of the command, not the thing under test); FRP Regulator = the active node, navy point + italic serif name + navy halo, carrying two role-colored PWM pin chips (violet --role-pwm dot + mono "PWM" label, displayOrder A then B). DIRECTED LINE: a single violet --role-pwm line runs PCM ‚Üí FRP Regulator (direction = command/current flowing DOWN to the coil; this is a controlled_by + electricalRole:'pwm' connection, so it animates). Its animation is the tell: it carries a PWM dash-flow whose pulse WIDTH tracks the scenario's wireState (pwm-low‚Üípwm-med‚Üípwm-high‚Üípwm-max) ‚Äî the wire itself visibly "thickens its on-time" as load climbs, so the canvas shows duty rising without a number. METER-HOOKUP OVERLAY: a hairline navy leader from the tapped PWM pin chip up to the Meter's top edge (one lead, back-probe) ‚Äî single-lead hookup, no across-two clamp, because PWM at-pin is a one-probe-to-the-signal measurement referenced to ground. GAUGE/TRACE: in the EXPECT column, a thin horizontal duty-ramp track (0%‚Äî‚Äî100%) with a soft lit window at the load-appropriate band; in the NOW column, a small live square-wave trace whose mark-space ratio matches the wireState ‚Äî the trace IS the gauge here, replacing the fat tabular figure that voltage-DC uses. NO rectangles, NO paragraphs on canvas; type/location/theory all live in the Meter tap-sheet.
perShape.meterBlock: "Trace window" variant of the Meter (not the EXPECT/NOW/verdict number format that voltage-DC uses, not the suppressed-locate format). EXPECT = a duty RANGE rendered as a ramp track with the current-load window lit (driven by expectedValue+expectedTolerance IF surfaced from the dropped test_actions columns, e.g. expectedValue 60, expectedTolerance 15, expectedUnit '%' ‚Üí window 45‚Äì75%; if those are null, EXPECT degrades honestly to a qualitative "moderate‚Üíhigh with load" band drawn from expectedObservation + the wireState ramp, with NO fabricated percent). NOW = the live square-wave trace + the verbatim prose reading pin_scenario_readings.reading for (pinId, scenarioId) e.g. "PWM ¬∑ near max duty," self-labeled with the scenario "NOW ¬∑ Heavy Load." VERDICT = a modulation verdict, not a range verdict: "alive ¬∑ tracking" (PWM present and duty trending correctly with load) / "alive ¬∑ not tracking" (PWM present but flat across loads ‚Üí suspect command vs mechanical) / "dead" (flat 0V or flat 12V ‚Üí branch_logic: wire broken PCM‚Üîpin or driver issue, cross-check pin B). The failure tree (component_pins.missingLogic: "Flat at 12V or flat at 0V ‚Üí wire broken between PCM and this pin‚Ä¶ Cross-check the other pin (B)") stays COLLAPSED while the trace is alive and unfolds only on a dead/flat reading.

perShape.schemaMapping: observationMethod='waveform_capture' (test_actions.observation_method) + meterMode (test_actions.meter_mode, DROPPED by loader) = the shape + meter-face selector. The duty WINDOW wants test_actions.expectedValue/expectedUnit/expectedTolerance (real/text/real, ALL present in schema, ALL dropped by the loader SELECT at load-system-topology.ts:297-305) ‚Äî surfacing them is a loader-select delta, not a new model. EXPECT prose = component_pins.expectedReading + test_actions.expectedObservation. NOW = pin_scenario_readings.reading keyed (pinId,scenarioId) ‚Üí TopologyScenario.pinReadings[pinId]. Live trace + wire pulse = scenario_wire_states.wireState ‚àà pwm-low|pwm-med|pwm-high|pwm-max ‚Üí TopologyScenario.pinStates[pinId]. Directed line = component_connections electricalRole='pwm' + connectionKind='controlled_by', PCM‚ÜíFRP Regulator. Pin chips = component_pins.roleAbbreviation='PWM', displayOrder, name. Probe lead = component_pins.probeLocation. Failure tree = component_pins.missingLogic. Verdict/fork = branch_logic.{condition,verdict,nextAction,routesToTestActionId} ‚Üí TopologyTestAction.branches. Scenario/load = system_scenarios.{label,loadLevel} ‚Üí TopologyScenario. Provenance = component_pins.sourceProvenance ‚Üí PROVENANCE_LABEL map (topology-detail-panel.tsx). Amber = component_pins.labelGap + components.electricalContract.
gaps: No electrical SUB-QUANTITY column anywhere (volts vs ohms vs duty% vs Hz vs amps). Today the shape must infer 'this is duty/PWM' from observationMethod='waveform_capture' + roleAbbreviation='PWM'. MINIMAL propose: add test_actions.meterMode usage (already a column, just dropped) as the canonical sub-quantity carrier, e.g. meterMode='duty' ‚Äî surface it, do not add a new column.

The duty WINDOW (e.g. 45‚Äì75%) cannot render as a number because test_actions.expectedValue/expectedUnit/expectedTolerance are dropped by the loader SELECT. PROPOSE: add these 4 existing columns to load-system-topology.ts:297-305 and to TopologyTestAction. This is the single highest-leverage change and is a loader delta, not a model change.

No structured per-(pin,scenario) duty NUMBER or in-band boolean ‚Äî pin_scenario_readings.reading is free prose ('PWM ¬∑ near max duty'). The 'alive/tracking/dead' verdict and the failure-tree-unfold trigger therefore lean on scenario_wire_states.wireState (pwm-low..pwm-max, which IS structured and load-ordered) rather than parsing prose. If a precise 'tracking the wrong direction' verdict is wanted later, PROPOSE a curator-authored per-(pin,scenario) modulation flag ‚Äî out of scope now.

No waveform-reference / known-good-trace column. The EXPECT trace shape (square wave, frequency) is synthesized from wireState + the qualitative expectedReading; there is no stored reference waveform to overlay. Acceptable for V2; PROPOSE later only if scope-capture is added.
No hookup column (one-lead / across-two / clamp / scope). Defaulted to single-lead back-probe from probeLocation prose ('Back-probe pin A'). Fine for PWM-at-pin; no field needed now.
perShape.incompleteState: Honest, because PWM is the shape where a fabricated number would be the worst lie. If the dropped test_actions columns are NOT surfaced (today's reality), the EXPECT column shows NO percent ‚Äî it shows the qualitative duty-ramp band ('moderate at idle ‚Üí near-max under load') drawn from expectedReading + the wireState ramp, and the NOW column shows the verbatim prose 'PWM ¬∑ near max duty.' The VERDICT degrades to the modulation question it can actually answer from structured wireState data ‚Äî 'alive ¬∑ tracking' (duty visibly changed across the load scenarios) ‚Äî and never claims a numeric pass/fail it can't support. If even wireState is absent for a pin, the trace renders as 'PWM expected ¬∑ not yet captured' (still-line placeholder, not a fake square wave) and the verdict is suppressed to neutral. The amber 'needs field check' line names exactly what's missing from components.electricalContract ‚Äî 'PWM frequency, duty-cycle range, and coil resistance not yet captured' ‚Äî so the tech sees the tool drew the behavior from theory and knows precisely which numbers a field capture would pin down. Never a fabricated duty%.

perShape.sceneExample: 2018 F-250 6.7L, P0087 rail-pressure-low, Heavy Load scenario, probing FRP Regulator Pin A. Canvas: PCM (graphite) ‚Üí violet PWM line ‚Üí FRP Regulator (navy, active) with pins [‚óè PWM A][‚óè PWM B]; the line pulses near-solid (pwm-max wireState); FRP Sensor glows faintly as 'why we're here.' Meter rises: 'FRP REGULATOR ¬∑ PWM (PIN A) ‚Äî PROBE / where: Back-probe pin A at the FRP regulator connector (back of DS rail).' EXPECT = duty-ramp track with the high/near-max window lit (qualitative today; would show e.g. 70‚Äì90% if expectedValue/Tolerance were surfaced). NOW = a live square wave with wide on-time + prose 'PWM ¬∑ near max duty,' labeled 'NOW ¬∑ Heavy Load.' VERDICT chip: 'alive ¬∑ tracking' (duty climbed idle‚Üíheavy, the regulator is being commanded). Under NOW: 'from theory ¬∑ why?' (graphite). Flip the ribbon chip to Idle and the wire pulse narrows (pwm-low/med), the trace mark-space shrinks, NOW settles to 'PWM ¬∑ moderate duty (trimming return for idle pressure)' with a damped transition ‚Äî EXPECT band stays fixed as the reference. Tap 'why?': expands 'Drafted from how this system works on the 6.7 Power Stroke ‚Äî not yet verified against a wiring diagram for this exact truck. Wire color, pin number, PWM frequency, duty-cycle range and coil resistance aren't captured yet.' If instead the reading were flat ('0V' / dead), the trace flatlines, the verdict flips to 'dead,' and the failure tree unfolds: 'Flat at 12V or flat at 0V ‚Üí wire broken between PCM and this pin, OR PCM driver issue. Cross-check pin B to localize' ‚Äî and pin B lights as the next move.

perShape.shapeName: Scope Trace ‚Äî voltage waveform compared against a reference SHAPE (Tier 2)
perShape.triggeredBy: Code picks this shape automatically when the step's test_action has observationMethod === 'waveform_capture' (the literal enum value at schema.ts:878; it is the shape-selector). This is distinct from observationMethod === 'electrical_measurement_at_pin' (which selects the single-number voltage/resistance gauge). The electrical sub-quantity is read from the focus pin's roleAbbreviation: 'PWM' (component_pins.roleAbbreviation = 'PWM' on vcv-a/vcv-b/reg-a/reg-b) routes to a square/duty trace; 'SIG' (frp-signal) with an analog ramp routes to an analog-voltage trace. The live trace ENVELOPE is read from scenario_wire_states.wireState for the focus pin in the active scenario: the pwm-low|pwm-med|pwm-high|pwm-max classes (schema.ts:705) drive a square wave at rising duty; the signal-rest|signal-low|signal-med|signal-high|signal-pegged classes drive an analog ramp height. Tier 2 (scope, not a multimeter number) is itself implied by observationMethod='waveform_capture'.
perShape.screenContents: HERO: a trace window (the gauge becomes a scope screen) showing TWO superimposed traces for the focus pin ‚Äî a fixed graphite REFERENCE trace (what good looks like) drawn as the quiet under-layer, and the live NOW trace drawn over it in ink, labeled by the current scenario. The verdict is "does the live trace match the reference shape," not a number match. perShape.screenContents: HERO is a TRACE, not a single digit ‚Äî this is the one shape where "one fat number" is wrong, because a current waveform's verdict is its SHAPE. The screen shows: the lit focus circuit on canvas (PCM ‚Üí IMV/VCV solenoid pins A/B on top of the CP4.2, the two PWM control wires animated, the CP4/rail receded behind, FRP sensor as the faint "why we're here" anchor at 0.45). On tap of the PWM pin, the Meter rises with a two-lane instrument: a fixed pale EXPECT reference trace (the known-good pulse shape for THIS scenario) and the live NOW trace drawn over it in ink, so good-vs-bad reads by overlay the way a master tech holds a capture against a reference waveform. Beneath the trace sits ONE derived scalar in mono-tnum ‚Äî the duty % the trace resolves to (e.g. "DUTY ~62%" at medium-load) ‚Äî not as the hero but as the trace's caption, because the number is a summary of the shape, not the thing itself. The verdict is a shape-match read ("pulse train present ¬∑ duty climbs with load") not an in-range chip. Probe lead row reads "where: clamp around pin-A control wire at the VCV connector on top of the HP pump." Provenance "from theory ¬∑ why?" sits quiet under the scalar (TRAINING-CONFIRMED ‚Üí graphite default). Below fold: connector/2-wires-both-to-PCM/Operational Theori, and the amber "needs field check" carrying the real label_gap ("wire color, pin number ‚Äî not yet captured") + the IMV electrical_contract gap ("PWM frequency, duty-cycle range, coil resistance not yet captured"). The failure tree (missing_logic: "flat at 12V or flat at 0V ‚Üí wire broken PCM‚Üîpin, cross-check pin B") stays COLLAPSED while a pulse train is present; unfolds only on a flat/dead trace.
perShape.lightVocabDrawing: No rectangles. The IMV/VCV is a small point on the harness with a one-line serif name "Volume Control Valve" (the user-facing pin label) sitting on top of the receded CP4.2 mark ‚Äî drawn as mounted, the way it physically sits. Two role-colored directed lines run PCM ‚Üí pin-A and PCM ‚Üí pin-B in --role-pwm violet (currently undefined in app ‚Äî propose define), animated with the existing .wire.is-active dash-flow ONLY for the in-focus PWM wires; the flow direction reads PCM-out-to-solenoid (the command). THE HOOKUP OVERLAY IS THE DIFFERENTIATOR: instead of two probe leads landing on a pin, a single clamp-ring glyph encircles the pin-A control wire ‚Äî a thin navy open circle (the amp clamp jaw) drawn around the wire stroke with a short stub running off to a scope corner, so the tech sees the meter reads the wire by induction, not by touching a terminal. A hairline leader runs from the clamped wire up to the Meter's trace lane. The gauge is NOT a needle ‚Äî it is the dual-lane trace itself: a fixed pale reference pulse train (graphite, --vt-recede weight) with the live capture stroked over it in --vt-fg, both drawn as clean square-ish PWM pulses (rise/dwell/fall) so the eye reads duty as the proportion of "high" width. The derived duty scalar renders in --vt-font-mono tabular-nums directly under the trace baseline. Three-color discipline holds: graphite reference + graphite provenance (drafted default), amber only on the label_gap line at probe moment, red ONLY if the captured trace is flat/dead (out-of-range = wrong shape, not wrong number).
perShape.meterBlock: "Trace window" variant of the Meter ‚Äî NOT the EXPECT/NOW/verdict single-number format. Layout: a wide trace lane spanning the card (EXPECT reference pulse train fixed + NOW capture overlaid), the NOW lane self-labels its scenario ("NOW ¬∑ Engine Idle" / "NOW ¬∑ Heavy Load" folded into the lane header per the locked scenario-in-NOW-column rule). Under the lane, a compact scalar strip: EXPECT "PWM present ¬∑ duty rises with load" (from expected_observation / expected_reading prose) and NOW the derived duty "~62%" in mono-tnum, with a quiet shape-match verdict ("tracking" when a pulse train climbs with load; no decorative pill). When the four dropped test_actions columns are surfaced (loader-select change), EXPECT upgrades from prose to a real band: expectedValue + expectedUnit (e.g. value=50, unit="% duty") with expectedTolerance drawing the pale reference band width, and meterMode ("PWM duty" / "current A") labeling the lane. On scenario flip the EXPECT reference stays fixed and the NOW capture re-draws + the duty scalar settles with the damped 280ms tnum transition (key turning feel). On a flat/dead capture: the NOW trace flatlines red, the duty scalar reads "0% ‚Äî no pulse," and the missing_logic failure tree unfolds in place.

Scenario flip re-draws the live trace + settles duty with the tnum transition; reference stays fixed. Reduced-motion: trace dash-flow frozen, capture drawn static, duty snaps.
perShape.sceneExample: 2018 F-250 6.7L, P0087, step = waveform_capture on the Volume Control Valve (the IMV solenoid on top of the CP4.2 ‚Äî the marquee diesel actuator). Scenario: Medium Load. Canvas: PCM lit, two violet PWM wires running down to VCV pins A/B mounted on the receded CP4.2, FRP sensor faint at 0.45 ("why we're here"), rest of the 25-part fuel system receded to 0.16. The tech clamps pin-A's control wire ‚Äî the navy clamp-ring glyph sits around that wire with a stub to a scope corner; a hairline leader runs up to the Meter. The Meter rises as a trace window: fixed pale reference pulse train (drafted, graphite) with the live capture overlaid in ink, duty caption "~78%" mono-tnum, NOW lane labeled "NOW ¬∑ Medium Load," EXPECT reading "PWM activity ¬∑ duty cycle varies with engine load" (real expected_reading), verdict "tracking ‚Äî duty climbs with load." Under the scalar: "from theory ¬∑ why?" (graphite, TRAINING-CONFIRMED). Tap "why?": "Drafted from how the 6.7 Power Stroke works ‚Äî PWM frequency, duty-cycle range, and coil resistance not yet captured for this exact truck" (real electrical_contract) + amber "wire color and pin number not yet captured" (real label_gap). Flip the chip to Heavy Load ‚Üí capture re-draws near-max-width pulses, duty settles to "~92%", reference stays fixed. The missing_logic ("flat at 12V or flat at 0V ‚Üí wire broken PCM‚Üîpin, cross-check pin B") stays hidden because a pulse train is present ‚Äî it would only unfold if this VCV came up flat, pointing the tech to clamp pin B to localize.

perShape.shapeName: The Meter ‚Äî Electrical Tier 3-4 (graceful): amps/clamp, bidirectional command, Hz/duty-PWM, AC charging ripple, diode test, min/max capture
perShape.triggeredBy: observationMethod is the primary shape selector. This shape is picked for observationMethod === 'electrical_measurement_at_pin' (amps, Hz, duty, diode, min/max) and observationMethod === 'waveform_capture' (PWM/ripple traces, bidirectional active-command). It is the SAME canvas as the basic voltage-DC/resistance electrical shape ‚Äî it does not branch into a new screen. The Tier 3-4 SUB-MODE is selected, in priority order, from: (1) test_actions.meterMode (free text ‚Äî the real loader column, today dropped; carries 'amps'/'clamp'/'Hz'/'duty'/'AC-ripple'/'diode'/'min-max'/'bidirectional' as the sub-quantity discriminator), then (2) the scenario_wire_states.wireState enum on the pin's wire (pwm-low|pwm-med|pwm-high|pwm-max => duty/PWM trace; signal-pegged => high-frequency trace), then (3) keyword sniff of pin.expectedReading / component.electricalContract prose ('PWM', 'duty cycle', 'current draw', 'ripple', 'diode'). In THIS 6.7L scene the triggers fire on: VCV Pin A/B (vcv-a/vcv-b, roleAbbreviation 'PWM', readings 'PWM ¬∑ MAX duty'), FRP Regulator Pin A/B (reg-a/reg-b, 'PWM'), and the Lift Pump 12V pin under an amps/clamp test (electricalContract 'electric motor; voltage, current draw ... not yet captured'). AC charging ripple and diode test have NO component in this scene ‚Äî they degrade to the name+hookup+honest-gap skeleton (see incompleteState).
perShape.screenContents:
Same Meter screen as the basic electrical shape ‚Äî the diagram is the quiet backdrop, the reading is the hero ‚Äî but the EXPECT/NOW block is rendered in the sub-mode the data names, and the graceful path is built IN, never a fallback page.

THE LIT CIRCUIT (canvas, upper ~70%): only the few elements this test touches. For the VCV PWM test: PCM (the commander) -> two PWM control wires -> Volume Control Valve, with the CP4.2 HP pump it sits on drawn faintly as the mechanical context, and the FRP Sensor glowing at ~0.45 as the 'why we're here' P0087 anchor. Everything else (8 injectors, rails, lift pump, filters) receded to --vt-recede 0.16, drawn not deleted.

THE METER (bottom band, ~30% desktop / detented sheet mobile), led by the sub-quantity:
- Label row: 'VOLUME CONTROL VALVE ¬∑ PIN A' + a quiet step-type tag (PROBE).
- probeLocation lead (serif): 'Back-probe pin A at the volume control valve connector on top of the HP pump.'
- THE HERO, sub-mode-specific:
  ‚Ä¢ DUTY/PWM (vcv-a, fault-low): the EXPECT plate shows the COMMAND in mono-tnum if expectedValue is present ('~85 %' duty) OR, when it is null (the real case today), it HONESTLY shows the qualitative band 'HIGH‚ÜíMAX duty' with a small live duty-trace glyph (a square-wave whose mark:space ratio matches the wireState pwm-max), and NOW reads the prose 'PWM ¬∑ MAX duty ‚Äî PCM trying to build pressure'. A 'commanding now' verdict, not in-range/out-of-range, because a command is a behavior not a setpoint.
  ‚Ä¢ AMPS/CLAMP (lift pump current): EXPECT plate 'expectedValue expectedUnit' ('~7 A') when present; NOW the measured draw; a clamp-around-the-wire overlay on the canvas instead of two probe tips.
  ‚Ä¢ Hz / MIN-MAX: EXPECT shows value+unit+tolerance ('¬±' from expectedTolerance); NOW the captured frequency or the min|max pair as two mono-tnum cells.
  ‚Ä¢ AC RIPPLE / DIODE: when no component carries the data (this scene), the block degrades to name+hookup+'EXPECT ‚Äî needs field check' with the trace window suppressed (see incompleteState).
- Provenance caption under the value: 'from theory ¬∑ why?' (graphite, the default here ‚Äî all 25 parts are TRAINING-CONFIRMED).
- COLLAPSED below fold: connector / # wires / Operational Theori, and the failure tree (missingLogic) which stays hidden on a commanding/in-range reading and unfolds only on a wrong one.
perShape.lightVocabDrawing:
No rectangles, no cards on canvas. Marks only:
- Each part = a small role-colored point (the .node .plate::before dot) + a one-line Instrument-Serif name. The VCV is the active node: navy marker + navy ink + slight italic.
- The two PWM control wires from PCM to the VCV are drawn with --role-pwm (violet family) at .wire.is-active weight (3.5px + drop-shadow glow). Their dash-flow speed is driven by the wireState: pwm-max animates fast (the existing .wire.pwm-max 0.22s flow), pwm-low slow ‚Äî so the wire itself SHOWS duty climbing as you flip scenarios, before you read a number. Receded wires freeze (animation:none) at 0.16.
- METER-HOOKUP OVERLAY = the distinguishing Tier 3-4 mark: instead of the basic two-probe-tips, the hookup glyph is drawn where the test actually connects. For a back-probe duty test: two tiny probe ticks straddling pin A. For AMPS/CLAMP: a small open-jaw clamp ring drawn ENCIRCLING the 12V wire (current goes through the jaw, not the tips) ‚Äî the visual that teaches 'clamp the wire, don't break the circuit'. For a scope/waveform test: a single scope-lead tick at the pin with a ground-clip tick at the nearest ground point.
- THE TRACE/GAUGE lives in the Meter, not on the canvas: a small inline square-wave for duty (mark:space set by wireState), a sine for AC ripple, a needle-arc for amps, two stacked tnum cells for min|max. A hairline leader connects the active pin up to the Meter so the number is anchored to WHERE it comes from.
- Three-color ink only: graphite default, --role-pwm violet on the live PWM wire, navy on the active pin/halo, amber reserved for the 'needs field check' caption, red only if NOW is out of range.
perShape.meterBlock:
Sub-mode determines the EXPECT/NOW/verdict layout ‚Äî all driven by data, no per-case design:
- DUTY/PWM & BIDIRECTIONAL COMMAND: EXPECT = expectedValue+'%' if present, else qualitative duty band from wireState ('LOW ¬∑ MED ¬∑ HIGH ¬∑ MAX') with a square-wave glyph. NOW = pinReading prose. VERDICT is NOT in-range/out-of-range ‚Äî it is a behavior verdict: 'COMMANDING' (PWM present and tracking load) vs 'DEAD' (flat 0V/12V => missingLogic unfolds). This is the key graceful move: a command has no single 'right' number, so the meter judges presence+response, not a setpoint.
- AMPS/CLAMP: classic EXPECT (value+unit) vs NOW (measured) vs in-range/high/low, tolerance from expectedTolerance. Hookup note: 'clamp around the wire'.
- Hz: EXPECT value+unit ('Hz') vs NOW captured frequency vs in-range.
- MIN/MAX CAPTURE: EXPECT a band (low|high from expectedValue ¬± expectedTolerance), NOW two cells 'MIN __ / MAX __', verdict = both-within-band.
- AC RIPPLE: EXPECT 'low ripple' (a flat-ish sine), NOW captured mV-pp, verdict good/excessive ‚Äî degrades to suppressed when no data.
- DIODE TEST: EXPECT a forward-drop band (~0.4-0.7 V), reverse = OL; NOW the two readings; verdict good/shorted/open.
When expectedValue is null (the honest common case in this scene), the EXPECT plate NEVER blanks ‚Äî it shows the qualitative band + the trace glyph + the amber 'expected value needs field check' caption.

perShape.schemaMapping:
SHAPE SELECT: test_actions.observationMethod ('electrical_measurement_at_pin'|'waveform_capture') -> this shape. SUB-MODE: test_actions.meterMode (real free-text column, currently dropped by the loader SELECT) -> amps/clamp/Hz/duty/AC-ripple/diode/min-max/bidirectional; fallback to scenario_wire_states.wireState (pwm-* / signal-pegged) -> duty/PWM trace; fallback to keyword sniff of component_pins.expectedReading + components.electricalContract.
EXPECT plate: test_actions.expectedValue + expectedUnit + expectedTolerance (all real, all currently dropped) -> the numeric setpoint+band; when null, test_actions.expectedObservation + component_pins.expectedReading -> qualitative band + trace glyph.
NOW column: TopologyScenario.pinReadings[pinId] (pin_scenario_readings.reading) self-labeled by scenario tuple (system_scenarios.keyPosition/engineState/loadLevel for operation rows; .slug for fault rows fault-low/fault-high).
TRACE animation: scenario_wire_states.wireState enum -> square-wave mark:space + dash-flow speed (reuse existing .wire.pwm-low/med/high/max + .wire.signal-pegged in topology.css:298-302).
HOOKUP overlay: component_pins.probeLocation + component_pins.edge + observationMethod (clamp glyph for meterMode='clamp'/'amps'; scope-lead for waveform_capture).
FAILURE TREE: component_pins.missingLogic, gated by verdict; FORK: branch_logic {condition,verdict,nextAction} (routesToTestActionId currently dropped ‚Äî surface it to auto-light the next part).
HONEST GAPS: components.electricalContract + component_pins.labelGap -> amber 'needs field check' caption + see-source body.
PROVENANCE: component_pins.sourceProvenance via PROVENANCE_LABEL map (topology-detail-panel.tsx).
WIRE COLOR: component_connections.electricalRole ('pwm'|'12v'|'ground'|'signal'|'5v-ref'|'low-ref') -> --role-* token.
LOADER DELTA (propose, do not apply): add meterMode, expectedValue, expectedUnit, expectedTolerance to the testActions SELECT (load-system-topology.ts ~line 303-305) + add routesToTestActionId to the branch SELECT (~line 318-323). No model change, no migration ‚Äî just stop dropping existing columns.
TOKEN DELTA (propose): define the six --role-* tokens, --vt-recede, and --vt-amber-600 in app/globals.css :root (referenced by topology.css:136/199/251/283-288 but undefined today, so PWM wires render colorless).
gaps: meterMode is a FREE-TEXT column, not an enum ‚Äî the sub-mode discriminator (amps|clamp|Hz|duty|AC-ripple|diode|min-max|bidirectional) relies on curators typing consistent values. Either (a) author a small controlled vocabulary convention for meterMode, or (b) add the optional electrical_quantity enum (proposed) for strict typing. Prefer (a) ‚Äî no migration.

No structured duty/Hz/amps VALUE today: expectedValue/expectedUnit/expectedTolerance exist but are unpopulated for the PWM pins in this scene (the readings are prose 'PWM ¬∑ MAX duty'). The numeric EXPECT plate only fills once a curator captures the duty-cycle range / coil-current spec that electricalContract currently flags as 'not yet captured'. Until then the qualitative-band degradation is the steady state, not an error.

Bidirectional / active-command verdict ('COMMANDING' vs 'DEAD') needs a behavioral judgment that no boolean carries today ‚Äî it must be inferred from the wireState (pwm-* present = commanding; off/steady-12v/steady-gnd flat = dead) or authored. Recommend deriving from wireState, not a new column.

AC charging ripple and diode test have NO component or test_action in this fuel scene ‚Äî they are defined by the shape, not by data here. Proven only when a charging-system or diode-bearing scene is seeded.

scenario_wire_states is a real loaded relation but is omitted from the partial scene-data.json slice, so the duty-trace animation could not be visually confirmed against this exact file ‚Äî confirmed against the schema enum and the existing topology.css pwm-* animation classes instead.

The six --role-* tokens, --vt-recede, and --vt-amber-600 are referenced in topology.css but undefined in globals.css ‚Äî until defined, every PWM/role-colored wire renders with no stroke color. One-line :root fix, proposed not applied.
perShape.incompleteState: Never blank, never 'I do not know'. The graceful skeleton always renders four things from whatever exists: (1) the TEST NAME (test_actions.description / pin name), (2) the HOOKUP (probeLocation + the clamp/scope/probe overlay glyph ‚Äî drawn even with zero readings), (3) the EXPECT ‚Äî numeric if expectedValue present, else the qualitative band ('HIGH‚ÜíMAX duty', 'low ripple', '~0.4-0.7 V forward') from expectedObservation/expectedReading, else an honest amber 'EXPECT ‚Äî needs field check', and (4) the GAP, named precisely from electricalContract/labelGap: 'PWM frequency, duty-cycle range, and coil resistance not yet captured' as an amber caption, with the see-source path carrying the full unknowns list. For a sub-mode with no data at all (AC ripple / diode in this scene): the trace window is SUPPRESSED (an empty scope frame is noise), the EXPECT plate shows the qualitative band only, and the verdict chip reads 'capture to compare' ‚Äî the honest 'needs field check' state, not a fabricated number. The duty value never shows a fake '85%' it does not have; it shows 'PWM ¬∑ MAX duty' verbatim and lets the wire-trace carry the magnitude. Provenance stays graphite 'from theory' (the default) until a field capture flips a specific spec to amber-resolved or field-verified.
perShape.mobile: 375-414px hard gate, same Meter grammar. Ribbon 44px: '6.7 PSD ¬∑ P0087 [Key On‚ñæ]'. The lit VCV circuit (PCM -> 2 PWM wires -> VCV) auto-frames into the TOP ~45%, ‚â•48px pin targets, the PWM wires animate their duty so magnitude is felt before the number. The Meter is a non-modal detented bottom sheet (peek ~150px / half / full), NO scrim, canvas stays live above; the Turo camera-pan keeps the VCV above the sheet edge. PEEK shows the hero only: label 'VCV ¬∑ PIN A', EXPECT band ('HIGH‚ÜíMAX duty' + inline square-wave glyph, largest mono-tnum on the phone), NOW prose 'PWM ¬∑ MAX duty', provenance 'from theory ¬∑ why?' as a ‚â•44px tap. The duty/ripple trace glyph shrinks but stays inline (it IS the hero in this sub-mode). For AMPS/CLAMP the clamp-on-wire overlay scales but stays legible (clamp around the wire, not tips). DRAG to HALF reveals connector / # wires / Operational Theori; the failure tree (missingLogic, cross-check pin B) only appears on a DEAD verdict. The amber 'needs field check' caption (Hz/duty-range/coil-ohms not yet captured) is a ‚â•44px tap that expands the see-source inline. Scenario flip ('Idle'->'Heavy Load') settles the NOW prose and speeds the wire-trace with a damped 280ms tnum transition. Curator coverage is hidden on mobile (curator-only, desktop ribbon entry).
perShape.sceneExample: Step: probe the Volume Control Valve Pin A, 6.7L F-250, P0087, scenario 'Fault Sim: No Rail Pressure' (fault-low). observationMethod = electrical_measurement_at_pin, meterMode (once un-dropped) = 'duty', roleAbbreviation = 'PWM'. CANVAS: PCM -> two violet --role-pwm wires -> Volume Control Valve lit at full ink (active node, navy marker on pin A), the CP4.2 it mounts to drawn faintly, FRP Sensor glowing at 0.45 as the P0087 anchor; the 8 injectors + rails + lift pump receded to 0.16. The active PWM wire animates FAST (wireState pwm-max, 0.22s flow) so you SEE max duty before reading it. METER (risen from bottom): label 'VOLUME CONTROL VALVE ¬∑ PIN A' ¬∑ PROBE; lead 'Back-probe pin A at the volume control valve connector on top of the HP pump.' EXPECT plate: expectedValue is null today, so it shows the qualitative band 'HIGH‚ÜíMAX duty' with a tight square-wave glyph (mark:space ~85:15) instead of blanking; NOW: 'PWM ¬∑ MAX duty ‚Äî PCM trying to build pressure'; VERDICT chip: 'COMMANDING' (behavior verdict, not in-range ‚Äî a command has no setpoint). Caption: 'from theory ¬∑ why?' (graphite). Below the EXPECT, an amber caption from the IMV/VCV electricalContract pattern: 'duty-cycle range & coil resistance ‚Äî needs field check'. The failure tree stays collapsed (it IS commanding); if it had read flat 12V/0V, missingLogic unfolds: 'Flat => wire broken PCM->pin, or PCM driver; cross-check pin B to localize' and pin B lights as the ONE next part. Flip to 'Idle' and NOW settles to 'PWM ¬∑ moderate duty' while the wire-trace slows ‚Äî the EXPECT band holds as the reference.

perShape.triggeredBy: Code picks this shape automatically when the current step's test_action has observationMethod === "pressure_test_with_gauge" (one of the 9 schema enum values at schema.ts:876). That single value swaps the electrical hookup (back-probe/leads) for a fluid hookup (gauge teed into the line) and the wire-role coloring for fluid-line coloring. A second selector ‚Äî meterMode ‚Äî decides the gauge sub-face: meterMode in {"psi","kpa","bar"} ‚Üí a radial PSI dial (lift-pump supply ~55 psi; CP4 rail up to ~29,000 psi); meterMode in {"gpm","lpm","cc","cc/min"} ‚Üí a volume/flow bar (return flow). When expectedValue is null (the common case today ‚Äî see gaps) the shape still renders from observationMethod alone but degrades to a target-less gauge (see incompleteState). For the SAME (pin,scenario) keyed prose reading, the pressure shape never appears ‚Äî readings are TEXT only; the gauge target comes from test_actions, not pin_scenario_readings.
perShape.screenContents: HERO: a fluid pressure gauge as the bottom Meter's instrument block ‚Äî EXPECT (the target, e.g. "55 PSI") rendered as a small radial dial face with its needle resting at the target arc, sitting beside NOW (the live measured/observed value for this step+scenario). One verdict read: needle inside band = quiet "at target", needle low = "low ‚Äî supply starved", pegged = "over". On the canvas above: ONLY the few elements this pressure check involves ‚Äî for the supply test that is Fuel Tank ‚Üí Electric Lift Pump ‚Üí Primary Filter/Separator ‚Üí CP4.2 inlet, drawn as a directed fluid path (tank source to pump to filter), with the FRP Sensor glowing faintly off in the corner as the single "why we're here" symptom anchor (P0087). The receded backdrop (CP4 high side, both rails, 8 injectors, return circuit, regulator, IMV, PCM) stays drawn at --vt-recede (0.16) so the whole fuel system is present but quiet. The meter-hookup overlay is the spear of this shape: a small gauge glyph teed onto the supply line right at the point you tap (between pump-out and filter-in), with a hairline leader from that tee up to the Meter ‚Äî so the reading visibly comes from THAT spot in the line, not from nowhere. Dimmed below the fold in the Meter sheet: where-to-tee text (probeLocation), wire/line summary, location, Operational Theori. Provenance caption "from theory ¬∑ why?" sits under the NOW value. NO rectangles, NO paragraphs on canvas.
perShape.lightVocabDrawing: MARKS: each fuel component is a small role-colored point (fuel = the muted --role-fuel / --vt-elem-fuel green) + a one-line Instrument-Serif name; no card, no border. DIRECTED LINES: fluid-line connections render as flowing directed paths (the existing .wire path with a fuel stroke + dash-flow animation showing fuel direction tank‚Üípump‚Üífilter‚ÜíCP4); flow direction comes from connection.direction (unidirectional) and from‚Üíto ordering. Only the lines IN this pressure check flow; the rest freeze/recede. METER-HOOKUP OVERLAY (the distinctive bit vs the electrical shape): instead of probe-lead dots on a pin, a tee-in gauge glyph ‚Äî a small circle-with-needle icon drawn ON the supply line at the tap point, with a short branch stub to it (a mechanical gauge teed into the line). The glyph is the high-contrast geometry; tapping it is the gesture. A 1px dashed navy leader runs from the gauge glyph up to the Meter card's top edge so the number is anchored to its physical source. GAUGE/TRACE: the EXPECT side is a true radial dial face (tick ring, target arc highlighted in navy, needle at target) for pressure; for return-flow it is a horizontal volume bar with a target notch. NOW renders the measured value as a second needle/fill on the same face when numeric, else as mono prose beside it. Three-color ink: graphite needle/ticks = drafted target (default), navy = the in-range/target band + field-verified, red needle zone = measured out-of-range only (the fault-low flat-low / fault-high pegged).
perShape.meterBlock: EXPECT / NOW / verdict, gauge-faced. EXPECT column: a radial dial (or flow bar) showing the target ‚Äî "55 PSI" big mono tnum with the needle parked on the target arc; tolerance (expectedTolerance) drawn as the green/navy band width around the arc. NOW column labeled "NOW ¬∑ Engine Idle" (scenario folded into the column header per the locked spec), showing the live value: when a numeric measurement exists it animates a second needle onto the same face; today, when only the prose pin reading exists, NOW shows the keyed (pin,scenario) text (e.g. lift-pump 12V-driven supply context) or, for a pure pressure step, "‚Äî tap to log reading" honestly. Verdict: at-target (quiet, navy tick) / low / over ‚Äî a single meter verdict tied to the needle position, NOT a decorative pill. missingLogic (pin.missingLogic) stays COLLAPSED while the needle is in-band and unfolds in place only when NOW is out-of-range (e.g. supply reads 20 psi ‚Üí "low supply ‚Üí restricted filter, weak/silent lift pump, or suction-side air leak"). For a LOCATE sub-step ("find where to tee the gauge") the whole gauge block is suppressed ‚Äî a number is noise when the job is finding the port.
