perShape.shapeName: Waveform (Scope) ‚Äî captured trace laid over the fixed known-good shape
perShape.triggeredBy: Code picks this shape automatically when the current step's test_action has observationMethod === 'waveform_capture' (the 4th of the 9 observationMethod enum values ‚Äî this enum IS the shape selector). Within waveform, the SHAPE of the trace and the gauge labels are sub-selected by two real signals, not invented: (1) the loaded TopologyScenario.pinStates[focusPinId] wireState enum ‚Äî if it is one of pwm-low|pwm-med|pwm-high|pwm-max the trace draws a SQUARE/duty-cycle waveform; if it is one of signal-rest|signal-low|signal-med|signal-high|signal-pegged it draws an ANALOG sweep; off|steady-12v|steady-gnd draw a flat line. (2) test_actions.meterMode (currently dropped by the loader) refines the y-axis label/unit (duty %, V, Hz). In the real 6.7L scene this fires on the FRP Regulator Pin A (reg-a) and Volume Control Valve Pin A (vcv-a) ‚Äî both PCM-modulated PWM solenoids ‚Äî and on the FRP Sensor Signal pin (frp-signal) as an analog sweep. If no waveform-method test_action exists for the focus pin, code never reaches this shape (it falls to electrical-measurement or scan_tool_pid).
perShape.screenContents: HERO is the scope trace window, not a single number. The bottom Meter band holds an oscilloscope face: one trace window where the KNOWN-GOOD reference shape is fixed on screen (PicoScope convention) as a quiet graphite ghost trace, and the CAPTURED trace is drawn over it in navy ink so good-vs-actual reads by perception, not memory. Above the window, a label row: "FRP REGULATOR ¬∑ PIN A ¬∑ PWM" + step chip "SCOPE". Below the window, the gauge readout strip: EXPECT (e.g. "~50% duty ¬∑ 100 Hz square") vs NOW (the captured summary, e.g. "48% duty, clean edges ¬∑ Idle") + a single verdict mark (matches-shape / off-shape). Under the NOW value sits the quiet provenance caption "from theory ¬∑ why?". The canvas above shows ONLY the waveform circuit: the FRP Regulator node lit on its real connector, the two PWM control wires running back to the PCM drawn in the pwm role color, the scope hookup overlay (scope-ground clip + signal probe drawn where the leads actually go on Pin A), and the FRP Sensor faint in the corner as the "why we're here" anchor. The other ~20 fuel parts are receded to the --vt-recede floor, still drawn. A hairline leader runs from the probed pin up to the trace window so the diagram stays load-bearing. The failure tree (missingLogic / branch_logic) stays COLLAPSED while the captured trace matches the reference shape.
perShape.lightVocabDrawing: No rectangles, no paragraphs on canvas. MARKS: the FRP Regulator drawn as a small actuator point (9px role-marked node) + one-line Instrument-Serif name; its two control pins as role-colored chips (Pin A / Pin B carry the --role-pwm violet dot). DIRECTED LINES: two pwm-role wires from PCM to the regulator pins, the active probed wire (Pin A) at .wire.is-active weight (3.5px + currentColor glow) with the dash-flow animation ON because a waveform IS a live signal; Pin B's wire focus-weight but not flowing; receded harness frozen at --vt-recede. METER-HOOKUP OVERLAY: a small scope-probe glyph clipped onto Pin A (signal lead) and a ground-clip glyph on the regulator/engine ground ‚Äî drawn AT the real lead positions, not abstractly, so the tech sees exactly where the scope leads go. THE GAUGE = the trace window itself: a thin-ruled scope graticule (NOT a dotted react-flow grid ‚Äî engraved hairlines on the bone surface), a time x-axis and amplitude y-axis, the fixed graphite REFERENCE waveform shape (square for PWM, sweep for analog) as the known-good band, and the captured navy trace overlaid. The shape of both traces is generated from the wireState enum: pwm-max => high-duty square, pwm-low => low-duty square, signal-pegged => flat-near-top analog line, signal-rest => low-flat. Mono tabular-nums for every axis tick and the duty/voltage readout so digits never jitter on scenario flip.
perShape.meterBlock: TRACE WINDOW mode (the waveform-specific Meter, distinct from PROBE's EXPECT/NOW number pair and LOCATE's suppression). The bottom Meter is an oscilloscope face: (1) the fixed reference trace (graphite ghost, the known-good shape, always on screen as the band to beat); (2) the captured trace (navy, drawn over it); (3) a gauge strip beneath: EXPECT = test_actions.expectedValue + expectedUnit rendered as the shape's defining numbers ("~50% duty ¬∑ 100 Hz"), NOW = the captured summary derived from pinStates wireState + scenario label ("48% duty ¬∑ Idle"), verdict = a single matches-shape (graphite/navy) or off-shape (red) mark ‚Äî phrased as shape comparison, never a decorative pill. On an out-of-shape capture (e.g. fault-low scenario where reg-a reads pwm-low when EXPECT is moderate, or fault-high where frp-signal is pegged-near-5V), the verdict turns red and the failure tree from branch_logic.condition/verdict/nextAction + pin.missingLogic unfolds in place ("flat or wrong-duty trace? ‚Üí check the PWM driver circuit and the regulator coil"). Two-pin compare is native here: tapping Pin B docks its trace as a second overlay so complementary A/B PWM can be read together (the data literally says "complementary to pin A"), capped at two so it stays a scope not a table.

The DRAWN GEOMETRY of both traces comes from TopologyScenario.pinStates[pinId] = scenario_wire_states.wireState (ALREADY LOADED): pwm-low|med|high|max map to square waves of increasing duty; signal-rest|low|med|high|pegged map to an analog line at increasing amplitude (pegged = flat near rail). gaps: LOADER DROPS the four structured EXPECT columns (meterMode, expectedValue, expectedUnit, expectedTolerance). Without them the reference trace has no authored amplitude/frequency/tolerance and the gauge can only label from prose. FIX = add these four to the testActions SELECT in load-system-topology.ts:298 and to TopologyTestAction. NO structured measured/captured trace is stored. tech_outcomes (measured_value/measured_unit/measured_observation/verdict) is the right home but is NOT loaded by this loader and NOT in Drizzle defs. The off-shape verdict trigger needs a per-(pin,scenario) in/out-of-tolerance signal. perShape.shapeName: Look / Inspect ‚Äî the good-vs-bad compare (visual / audible / touch / smell, no number)
perShape.triggeredBy: Code selects this shape automatically from test_actions.observationMethod ‚àà {direct_visual_internal, direct_visual_external, audible, touch, smell} ‚Äî five of the nine enum values, the whole non-instrument family. The sub-quantity that further forks the LAYOUT (not the shape) is which of the five it is: direct_visual_* ‚Üí side-by-side good/bad swatch pair; audible ‚Üí a two-state "what you should hear vs what you shouldn't" with a quiet waveform-rest glyph (no scope trace, just the sound description); touch ‚Üí a "should feel / a fault feels" pair (heat, pulsing, wetness); smell ‚Üí a "should / fault smells of" pair (diesel vs coolant-sweet, burnt). The discriminator is observationMethod ONLY ‚Äî these five rows have expectedValue/expectedUnit/expectedTolerance/meterMode all NULL in the data, which is itself the signal there is no gauge to draw; expectedObservation (the one expectation field the loader surfaces today) is non-null and carries the GOOD state. In the 6.7L scene: "inspect the primary fuel filter for fine metallic glitter / CP4 debris" ‚Üí direct_visual_internal; "check the water separator bowl for free water" ‚Üí direct_visual_external; "listen for the lift-pump prime whir at key-on" ‚Üí audible.
perShape.screenContents: HERO: a single good-vs-bad COMPARE pair where the gauge would be ‚Äî two facing states, GOOD (what it should look/sound/feel/smell like) on the left, FAULT (the failure signature) on the right, with the verdict reading as "which one are you looking at?" not "what number did you get?". There is no EXPECT-number plate and no NOW digits ‚Äî that whole instrument block is swapped out, because these rows have no expectedValue/Unit/Tolerance. The compare pair is fed by expectedObservation (GOOD) and the branch_logic fail-condition prose (FAULT). The supporting marks on the canvas: the inspected part lit on its REAL place in the fuel path (the Primary Filter / Water Separator point), with the directed fuel-flow line through it drawn quietly so the tech sees WHERE in the circuit this thing sits (tank ‚Üí lift pump ‚Üí [FILTER] ‚Üí CP4) ‚Äî the filter is the choke point the debris/water shows up at. The active part gets the navy marker; tank, lift pump and CP4 stay drawn but receded as the "why this spot matters" context (debris here implicates the CP4 upstream-vs-downstream story). DIMMED / below the fold in the sheet: location ("driver-side frame rail, downstream of lift pump"), what-it-means (the CP4 catastrophic-pump reasoning from component.body), and the provenance caption. The failure tree (the branch ‚Äî debris found ‚Üí CP4 failed, replace pump + flush system) stays COLLAPSED unless the tech marks "fault seen"; on a clean inspection it never unfolds.
perShape.lightVocabDrawing: MARKS: the inspected part is a small role-colored point + a one-line Instrument-Serif name on the fuel spine ‚Äî no rectangle, no card. Because this is a fluid/mechanical inspection, the point sits ON the directed FUEL-FLOW line (warm-muted --role-fuel stroke), not on a wire. DIRECTED LINES: the fuel path is drawn left-to-right with a single subtle flow direction (tank ‚Üí lift pump ‚Üí FILTER ‚Üí CP4); only the segment INTO the filter and OUT toward the CP4 is in focus tier (1.4‚Äì2.4px), the rest receded to --vt-recede (0.15). No dash-flow animation on a look/inspect step ‚Äî nothing is "live current"; the flow line is a static directed hairline (the prototype already gates wire-flow to probe-step + 12v role, so look steps correctly show no animation). METER-HOOKUP OVERLAY: there is NO meter hookup ‚Äî instead the slot the leads would occupy is replaced by an "eye / hand / ear / nose" inspection marker at the part (a tiny mono micro-cap verb: LOOK / LISTEN / FEEL / SMELL drawn where the probe-leads label sits in the probe shape), so the affordance reads "put your eyes here" the way the probe shape reads "put your leads here." GAUGE/TRACE REPLACEMENT: the gauge face is replaced by the good-vs-bad COMPARE ‚Äî two small engraved swatch frames side by side (GOOD | FAULT), each a hairline 0.5px frame with serif description inside, the FAULT frame keyed with the three-color discipline (graphite while drafted/unobserved; flips to red --vt-risk-high only if the tech marks the fault seen). For audible the swatch is a flat signal-rest glyph (a quiet horizontal line, NOT a scope trace) beside the sound words; for smell/touch the swatch is purely typographic (no fake icon). Leader hairline runs from the lit part up to the compare, same as the probe shape's pin‚ÜíMeter leader, so the diagram stays load-bearing.
perShape.meterBlock: The bottom Meter is in its "good-vs-bad compare" mode (the brief's explicit third Meter mode alongside EXPECT/NOW-verdict and trace-window). Layout: header = part label ("PRIMARY FILTER ¬∑ WATER SEPARATOR") + step type chip "LOOK" (or LISTEN/FEEL/SMELL). Lead line = the inspection instruction from test_actions.description ("Inspect the filter media and bowl for fine metallic glitter or debris"), led by a mono micro-cap "look"/"listen" the way PROBE leads with "where". Then, in place of EXPECT/NOW, the COMPARE: GOOD (from expectedObservation: "Clean amber/clear fuel, no metallic sheen, dry separator bowl") set against FAULT (from branch_logic.condition prose: "Fine silver glitter or grey paste in the media = CP4 grenaded ‚Äî metal is everywhere downstream"). The verdict is not a chip with a number ‚Äî it is a single tap target the tech sets: "looks good" / "fault seen", because this reading is a human judgment, not an instrument value. Provenance caption sits under the compare ("from theory ¬∑ why?", graphite/neutral). Failure tree (branch_logic.nextAction / routesToTestActionId) stays collapsed until "fault seen" is set, then unfolds the ONE next move (CP4 failed ‚Üí replace pump + flush entire system, do not just swap the filter) ‚Äî never previewed.

perShape.schemaMapping: PRIMARY DISCRIMINATOR: test_actions.observationMethod ‚Äî the look/inspect shape fires on the five non-instrument enum values. The four meter columns (meterMode, expectedValue, expectedUnit, expectedTolerance) are NULL for these rows by construction ‚Äî that NULL IS the signal "there is no gauge," so the gauge slot is replaced by the compare rather than left empty. COMPARE PAIR: GOOD = test_actions.expectedObservation (the single expectation field load-system-topology already surfaces as TopologyTestAction.expectedObservation ‚Äî zero loader change needed for the GOOD half). FAULT = TopologyTestAction.branches[] where verdict='fail' ‚Üí branches[].condition is the failure-signature prose; branches[].nextAction is the collapsed next move. CANVAS PLACEMENT: connections[] filtered to connectionKind='fluid-line' touching this component draws the directed fuel-flow line (in the scene: Fuel Tank‚ÜíElectric Lift Pump‚ÜíPrimary Filter‚ÜíBosch CP4.2); component.location/name/kind from the loaded TopologyComponent label the mark; component.body supplies the dimmed reasoning. PROVENANCE: all 25 components + 9 pins are TRAINING-CONFIRMED ‚Üí PROVENANCE_LABEL maps to "from theory" (graphite/neutral default, not amber). LOADER DELTAS TO PROPOSE (do not apply): (1) add test_actions.sourceProvenance to the loader SELECT so per-inspection provenance is honest (component provenance ‚âÝ action provenance); (2) add branch_logic.routesToTestActionId to the branches projection so the fault fork can route to the next test_action; (3) the GOOD/FAULT compare and the lead line need NO new columns ‚Äî they ride expectedObservation + branch_logic + connections that are already loaded. amber "needs field check" register comes ONLY from real component.electricalContract gaps (e.g. WIF sensor "wire count and signal type not yet captured") and labelGap ‚Äî surfaced ONLY inside the see-source pull, never standing on the canvas.
gaps: NO per-(component,action) FAULT-signature column distinct from the branch: today the FAULT half is reconstructed from branch_logic.condition (verdict='fail') prose. That works for the scene (CP4-debris and water-in-bowl both have a fail branch), but a look step with no authored fail branch would render only the GOOD half. Propose: rely on branch_logic verdict='fail' as the canonical FAULT source; do NOT add a new column ‚Äî author the fail branch as curator data when missing.

NO structured verdict capture for a human judgment: a look has no number, so 'looks-good/fault-seen' has nowhere to write. tech_outcomes (measured_observation:text, verdict:text) is the right home but is NOT loaded and NOT in Drizzle defs. Propose: surface tech_outcomes for write-back, or keep the call as transient UI state for V2 staging until outcomes are wired.

branch_logic.routesToTestActionId (the fork target) is DROPPED by the loader SELECT ‚Äî the FAULT tree can name the next move (nextAction) but cannot ROUTE to it. Propose: add routesToTestActionId to the loader branches projection.

test_actions.sourceProvenance is DROPPED by the loader ‚Äî provenance shown on a look step currently falls back to the component's provenance, which can over- or under-state confidence in the specific inspection. Propose: surface it (minimal SELECT change).
NO explicit good-vs-bad PAIR ordering flag: which branch is the 'good outcome' vs 'fault' is inferred from verdict enum (ok/warn vs fail). Sufficient for the scene; no new column needed.

When expectedObservation itself is missing, the sheet shows only the instruction (test_actions.description) and the lit part with its fuel-flow line, and the compare is suppressed with "what good looks like ‚Äî not yet captured ¬∑ why?" (graphite, the see-source pull explains it's drafted-not-verified). perShape.sceneExample: 2018 F-250 6.7L, P0087, walk reaches the supply-side inspection before any teardown (low invasiveness, ordered early by priority). Step: "Inspect the primary fuel filter and water-separator" ‚Äî observationMethod=direct_visual_internal ‚Üí look/inspect shape fires. Canvas: the fuel spine is drawn tank ‚Üí lift pump ‚Üí [PRIMARY FILTER, lit navy] ‚Üí CP4.2, the filter on its --role-fuel flow line, lift pump + CP4 receded as context (because debris here is the CP4-grenaded story). Bottom Meter, compare mode: header "PRIMARY FILTER ¬∑ WATER SEPARATOR" + chip "LOOK"; lead "look ‚Äî inspect the filter media and separator bowl"; COMPARE ‚Üí GOOD (expectedObservation): "Clean amber/clear diesel, no metallic sheen, separator bowl dry and clear." FAULT (branch_logic verdict=fail condition): "Fine silver glitter or grey paste in the media, or water/cloudy fuel in the bowl." Verdict = a human call: [looks good] / [fault seen]. Under it, graphite "from theory ¬∑ why?". Dimmed below: location "driver-side frame rail, downstream of lift pump" and the reasoning from CP4.body ‚Äî "the CP4 is fuel-lubricated with no oil circuit; metallic debris in the filter means it has grenaded and seeded metal through the whole system." If the tech sets "fault seen," the FAULT swatch flips red and the ONE next move unfolds (branch nextAction): "CP4 failed ‚Äî replace the pump AND flush the entire fuel system, do not just change the filter." If "looks good," nothing unfolds and the walk advances silently to the next test. A second look step in the same scene ‚Äî "listen for the lift-pump prime whir at key-on" (audible) ‚Äî renders the same shape with a sound-rest glyph instead of swatches: GOOD "brief whir for ~2s then silence," FAULT "no whir at all = no power/ground or dead pump motor."

perShape.shapeName: Fork / Decision ‚Äî the reading resolves to one road, only that road lights
perShape.triggeredBy: This is NOT a fresh `observationMethod` shape ‚Äî a fork is the AFTERMATH of any probe/pressure/look step once its reading lands. Code selects it when the just-completed test_action has `branch_logic` rows AND the tech's NOW reading matched one row's `condition`, resolving a `verdict`. Specifically: the matched `branch_logic.verdict` is one of the real enum values `ok | warn | fail | impossible`, and the matched row carries a non-null `routesToTestActionId` (currently DROPPED by the loader ‚Äî the #1 surface for this shape). The reading that armed the fork is most often `observationMethod = electrical_measurement_at_pin` (the lift-pump 12V back-probe: "12V steady" vs "0V when commanded") or `pressure_test_with_gauge` (supply ~55 psi vs flat). In THIS scene the live split is `verdict=ok` on the supply side ‚Üí route to the high-pressure side test_action, vs `verdict=fail` (0V) ‚Üí route upstream to relay/fuse. The fork screen renders the instant a verdict resolves; if NO branch_logic row matches the prose reading, it stays NEUTRAL and shows no fork (honest "verdict undetermined").
perShape.screenContents: The hero is NOT a new big number ‚Äî it is the verdict the last reading produced, and the single road that verdict opens. Top of the Meter band reads back the test that just resolved as a settled line: "LIFT PUMP ¬∑ 12V POWER" with the NOW value carried forward ("12V steady ¬∑ Key On") rendered quiet/grey (it's history now, not the live hero), and beside it the resolved verdict chip ‚Äî graphite "in range" for ok, red only for fail/measured-wrong. The loud element is one short directed line of consequence drawn from `branch_logic.nextAction`: the chosen road. On canvas, the supply-side cluster the tech just cleared (Lift Pump, relay, filter) recedes one notch toward `--vt-recede` and the ONE next part the route points at lights up and slides toward focus ‚Äî for `verdict=ok` that is the CP4.2 / FRP-regulator high-pressure side igniting as the new focus; for `verdict=fail` (0V) it is the relay+fuse upstream lighting. The unpicked road is never drawn (no ghosted alternative, no "or else", no count). A hairline directed leader animates from the cleared pin to the newly-lit part so the eye sees the baton pass. Below the fold, dimmed: the one-line reasoning ("supply pressure is good, so the loss is downstream ‚Äî go to the high-pressure side") and the provenance caption.
perShape.lightVocabDrawing: No rectangles, no decision-diamond, no branching tree. The fork is drawn as a baton-pass along the harness, not a flowchart node. (1) MARKS: the resolved component keeps its small role-colored point but its serif name desaturates toward `--vt-fg-3`; the newly-chosen next component's point brightens to focus ink and its serif name comes up to `--vt-fg`, gaining the navy `.node.active` halo. (2) DIRECTED LINE: a single animated leader ‚Äî reuse `.wire.is-active` (3.5px + `drop-shadow(0 0 6px currentColor)`) ‚Äî runs from the just-cleared pin/part to the next part, colored by the next leg's role (`--role-12v` warm-red if routing upstream to relay power, `--role-fuel` muted-green if routing along the supply line to the high-pressure side). It draws on (stroke-dashoffset sweep) over `--vt-dur-3` so the route is felt as motion, a current finding its path. The OLD focus circuit's wires drop to `.wire.off`/recede in the same beat. (3) NO METER-HOOKUP OVERLAY here ‚Äî the fork is post-measurement; leads are off the part. The hookup overlay returns on the NEXT screen (the routed-to test). (4) GAUGE/TRACE: no live gauge; instead the carried-forward reading sits as a settled tabular-figure value (mono, tnum) gone quiet, with the verdict chip as the only instrument mark ‚Äî the gauge has been read, the needle has settled, now it points the way.
perShape.meterBlock: SUPPRESSED EXPECT/NOW as a live instrument ‚Äî this is the "branch step" budget. There is no fresh EXPECT band because nothing new is being measured; the prior reading is shown only as settled history (grey carried-forward NOW, no expected-band frame). The Meter format becomes VERDICT ‚Üí ROUTE: line 1 = the resolved verdict from `branch_logic.verdict` ("Supply side checks out" for ok / "No power at the pump" for fail), line 2 = the consequence drawn verbatim-ish from `branch_logic.nextAction` ("Move to the high-pressure side ‚Äî CP4.2 and the rail regulator" / "Power's missing ‚Äî check the lift-pump relay and fuse upstream"). The verdict chip uses three-color discipline strictly: graphite for ok/warn (drafted-path, neutral), red `--vt-risk-high`/`--vt-risk-destructive` ONLY when `verdict=fail` (measured-wrong). `verdict=impossible` reads as a quiet graphite "that reading shouldn't happen ‚Äî recheck the hookup," never red. The unpicked branches' conditions/verdicts are NOT listed. One tap down (dimmed) sits the reasoning line from `branch_logic.reasoning` (MISSING from loader today) as the see-source "why this road?" pull.

perShape.schemaMapping: Fork = a `test_actions` row whose `branch_logic` children resolved. ORDER of which test ran first = `symptom_test_implications.priority` (higher first) ‚Äî unchanged. The chosen road = the single `branch_logic` row whose `condition` the tech's NOW reading satisfied; that row's `routesToTestActionId` ‚Üí the next `test_actions.id` ‚Üí `testActions.componentId` (+ any `component_pins` on that component, ordered by `displayOrder`) is the part that lights. Verdict chip = `branch_logic.verdict`. Route line = `branch_logic.nextAction`. See-source = `branch_logic.reasoning`. Carried-forward reading = `pin_scenario_readings.reading` keyed (focusPinId, activeScenarioId) via `scenario.pinReadings`. Next leg's wire color = `component_connections.electricalRole` of the edge into the routed-to part (‚Üí the `--role-*` token), or `connectionKind=fluid-line` ‚Üí `--role-fuel`. Provenance caption = `branch_logic.sourceProvenance` through the existing PROVENANCE_LABEL map. Animation: the cleared circuit's edges flip from `.wire.is-active`‚Üí`.wire.off`; the new leg gets `.wire.is-active`. Reuses topology-flow.ts toFlowElements wire classes and TopologyNode (with the proposed `tier` prop). NOT loaded today: `tech_outcomes` (measured_value/verdict) would be the eventual real measured fork-resolution store, but the fork screen runs on branch_logic alone.
gaps: routesToTestActionId is the single hard blocker: the loader's branch_logic SELECT drops it, so today branches[] = {condition,verdict,nextAction} with NO target id. The fork can render the verdict + the prose nextAction line, but the CANVAS cannot automatically light the correct next part without it. Fix = add routesToTestActionId (and reasoning) to the loader SELECT + the TopologyTestAction.branches[] type. This is a loader-select delta, not a model change ‚Äî propose to Brandon, do not apply.

No deterministic condition matcher: branch_logic.condition is free text ('0V when commanded', 'supply pressure good') and the NOW reading is free prose. Picking WHICH branch fired by string-matching is fragile. Minimal fix = surface the 4 already-dropped test_actions structured columns (expectedValue/expectedUnit/expectedTolerance/meterMode) so in/out-of-range is computed from a real number, and/or add an explicit comparator+threshold to branch_logic. Prefer surfacing the dropped columns over inventing fields.

No measured-result capture: tech_outcomes (measured_value, verdict) is the real place a tech's actual reading + the resolved verdict would persist, but it is unloaded and not in Drizzle defs. The prototype fork is theory-only (which road the system WOULD pick); a field-resolved fork needs tech_outcomes wired. Out of scope for this shape's render, flagged for the build.
branch_logic.reasoning is nullable and dropped ‚Äî if absent, the 'why this road?' see-source pull must honestly collapse (show nextAction only), not fabricate a rationale.

(3) verdict resolves but reasoning is NULL ‚Üí the 'why this road?' pull simply doesn't appear; the route line stands alone. Provenance always present: a routed road carries 'from theory ¬∑ why?' (graphite) since every branch here is TRAINING-CONFIRMED ‚Äî it never poses as field-verified.

The Meter settles: line 1 grey '12V steady ¬∑ Key On' with a graphite 'in range' chip; line 2 loud, from nextAction: 'Supply side checks out ‚Äî move to the high-pressure side, CP4.2 and the rail regulator.' On canvas the Lift Pump / relay / filter recede toward 0.15 and a muted-green (`--role-fuel`) leader sweeps along the supply line to the CP4.2 HP Pump, which brightens to focus with its navy halo as the new active part; the FRP regulator comes up beside it. perShape.shapeName: Locate / Orient ‚Äî find the part, reading band suppressed
perShape.triggeredBy: There is no real observationMethod enum value for "locate" today ‚Äî that is the core gap. The 9 enum values (scan_tool_pid | pressure_test_with_gauge | electrical_measurement_at_pin | waveform_capture | direct_visual_internal | direct_visual_external | audible | touch | smell) all describe a MEASUREMENT or sensory test, never a pure "go find this physical thing first" step. So the shape is selected by ONE of two real triggers: (1) the step's test_action has observationMethod = 'direct_visual_external' AND invasiveness = 1 (lowest) AND expectedObservation describes a location/identification rather than a condition ‚Äî the closest existing approximation, but ambiguous; or (2) PROPOSED: a minimal test_actions.stepKind = 'locate' flag (see gaps) that authoritatively forces this shape. The honest read: today the renderer cannot reliably auto-pick LOCATE from the existing enum without prose-guessing. The prototype hard-codes type:"locate" on the step object precisely because the column does not exist. Lead selector is still observationMethod; LOCATE is the one shape the current enum cannot express, so it needs the smallest possible authored signal.
perShape.screenContents: The HERO is the location string, not a number. The screen shows: the lift-pump cluster lit on the real fuel spine ‚Äî Electric Lift Pump (component.location "driver-side frame rail") as the active node, with its ground edge drawn down to the Chassis Ground stud; the fuel-in / fuel-out fluid lines kept faint so the part reads in physical context; everything else in the 25-part harness receded to the --vt-recede floor (0.15) but still drawn (fade-not-vanish). The bottom Meter band rises but its instrument block (EXPECT / NOW / verdict) is ENTIRELY SUPPRESSED ‚Äî m-instr display:none in the proto. In its place the band leads with the find-it prose: a "FIND" eyebrow (not "WHERE"/"PROBE") + the location sentence in serif italic ("Find the pump's ground stud ‚Äî driver-side frame rail, the chassis stud near the tank. Locate it before you meter it."). No EXPECT plate, no NOW value, no in-range chip, no verdict, no failure tree. Below the fold (one tap down): Location detail, the amber "needs field check" gap, and the Operational Theori. Dimmed/below-fold: the secondary facts row; the curator-only coverage panel (role-gated, never shown to techs). The active wire does NOT animate (locate steps freeze the dash-flow ‚Äî a number/flow is noise when the job is "find the thing").

The one directed line that matters is the GND edge: pump ‚Üí chassis stud, drawn in --role-ground graphite (return = neutral), held at focus opacity but with NO wire-flow animation (locate freezes it; movement implies measurement). The fuel-in (tank‚Üípump) and fuel-out (pump‚Üífilter) lines render faint as physical orientation. Crucially there is NO meter-hookup overlay and NO leader line to the band on a LOCATE step (#leader stays off when step.locateOnly) ‚Äî because there is no reading to anchor; the leader is a probe-shape affordance, suppressed here. NO gauge, NO trace, NO EXPECT plate. The single piece of "where" ink is the chassis-ground stud point itself, sitting at the physical end of the ground edge ‚Äî the drawing literally points at the thing to go find.
perShape.meterBlock: SUPPRESSED ‚Äî this is the defining property of the shape. The EXPECT / NOW / verdict instrument block is removed entirely (m-instr display:none). The reading-suppression means the four dropped test_actions columns (meterMode, expectedValue, expectedUnit, expectedTolerance) are IRRELEVANT to this shape ‚Äî LOCATE is the one shape that needs none of them, so it ships on the current loader SELECT with zero loader change for the reading path. test_actions.expectedObservation (the only expectation field loaded today) is also not shown on a locate step. component_pins.expectedReading is deliberately NOT rendered (suppressed). component_pins.missingLogic is NOT rendered (no failure tree on a locate). component_pins.labelGap ‚Üí the amber 'needs field check' fact. components.role/body ‚Üí Operational Theori. component_pins.sourceProvenance ‚Üí PROVENANCE_LABEL map ("from theory"). gaps: test_actions has NO stepKind / step-type column and there is NO step table ‚Äî the 9-value observationMethod enum (scan_tool_pid‚Ä¶smell) cannot express 'locate: find the part, suppress the reading.' PROPOSE the minimum: a nullable test_actions.stepKind text column ('locate'|'orient'|'probe'|'interpret'|'branch'). With stepKind absent today, the renderer can only APPROXIMATE LOCATE from observationMethod='direct_visual_external' + invasiveness=1 + prose inspection of expectedObservation ‚Äî unreliable. Until stepKind exists, the prototype's hard-coded type:'locate' is the only honest source.

perShape.triggeredBy: This is the ONLY shape NOT picked from a test_action's observationMethod ‚Äî it is the session-entry screen, selected by state (no step advanced yet: SelectionState has no focusPinId / activeStep). perShape.screenContents: HERO = the complaint stated as a verdict-already-rendered, not a question. Top line, serif: "P0087 ¬∑ Rail pressure too low" with the recessed mono fault state next to it: "MIL ON ¬∑ low power under load". The hero element is a single quiet reading block in The Meter band: EXPECT (what rail pressure SHOULD do) vs NOW (the FRP PID the truck is actually reporting ‚Äî "below PCM threshold") with a RED verdict already lit, because unlike every later step this reading is the customer-reported fact, not a fresh probe ‚Äî the truck already failed it. The canvas above shows the SINGLE whole-system glimpse the brief allows for this one screen only: the fuel spine drawn end-to-end (tank ‚Üí lift pump ‚Üí filters ‚Üí CP4.2 ‚Üí rail ‚Üí injectors) at a uniform quiet graphite, with exactly ONE element elevated ‚Äî the FRP Sensor, flagged "why we're here" ‚Äî because the complaint is "the number this sensor reports is wrong." No circuit is narrowed yet (narrowing is the NEXT screen's job); this is the only screen that earns the full map. Dimmed/below-fold: probeLocation, the failure tree (missingLogic), connector/wire facts, Operational Theori ‚Äî all in the tap-sheet, collapsed. There is NO "next step" preview, NO "step 1 of N".
perShape.lightVocabDrawing: NO rectangles. The fuel spine is drawn as role-colored directed lines left‚Üíright along the mid-spine: fluid-line edges (--role-fuel, muted) carry the fuel path with the flow-dash animation running gently end-to-end (this is the one screen where the whole flow animates, signalling "the system as a whole, before we narrow"). Each part is a small graphite point (9px hollow marker, --vt-bone-500 ring) + a one-line Instrument-Serif name; all at the same recede-ish weight EXCEPT the FRP Sensor, which gets the navy active marker (--vt-signal-500 ring + halo) and a thin mono "why we're here" flag tucked above it on a --vt-bg knockout. A single hairline leader (--vt-signal-500, dashed 2 3, opacity .5) runs from the FRP Sensor's SIG pin down to the top edge of The Meter band ‚Äî tying the lit part to the reading that proves the complaint, the same leader mechanic the probe shape uses, so the diagram reads as load-bearing not decorative. The reading itself sits in the band as a gauge face: a fat mono tabular-nums value with an EXPECT plate beside it; for the symptom PID the EXPECT plate shows the expected pressure band and NOW shows "below threshold." The CP4.2/rail/injectors stay drawn at the bone graphite floor (never display:none) so the tech sees the whole field they're about to walk into.
perShape.meterBlock: EXPECT / NOW / verdict ‚Äî but in its one special "complaint-already-failed" framing. EXPECT plate: the rail-pressure expectation (from the symptom PID's expected band ‚Äî e.g. "~rises with load, hundreds‚Äìthousands psi"), rendered mono tnum. NOW column: self-labels the scenario folded in ‚Äî "NOW ¬∑ Engine Cranking" ‚Äî and shows the reported PID prose: "Flat low even while cranking" (the real fault-low reading verbatim). Verdict chip: RED "below threshold" ‚Äî this is the ONE screen where red is lit at entry without a fresh probe, because the complaint is a confirmed out-of-range fact the truck reported, not a we-don't-know-yet. Provenance caption beneath, quiet graphite: "from theory ¬∑ why?" (the EXPECT band is drafted; the NOW value is the customer/scan-tool fact). Crucially: the failure tree stays COLLAPSED here too ‚Äî this screen confirms WHAT is wrong, it does not yet branch into WHY (that is the first narrowing step that follows). Below-fold secondary (one tap): "what this sensor does ¬∑ what good looks like ¬∑ Operational Theori".

perShape.schemaMapping: Hero reading = scan_tool_pid path. observationMethod comes from the symptom's defining test_actions row (test_actions.observationMethod='scan_tool_pid', componentId = FRP Sensor). EXPECT = the FOUR dropped test_actions columns (expectedValue:real, expectedUnit:text, expectedTolerance:real, meterMode:text) ‚Äî surface via a loader-SELECT change ONLY (no new model); fallback to component_pins.expectedReading (free text) when null. NOW = pin_scenario_readings.reading at PK(frpSignalPinId, faultScenarioId), exposed today as TopologyScenario.pinReadings[pinId]. The elevated part = components row (FRP Sensor, source_provenance=TRAINING-CONFIRMED ‚Üí "from theory"). Whole-system glimpse = the already-loaded components[]+connections[] (component_connections.connectionKind drives fluid-line vs electrical-wire; electricalRole non-null drives animated edge). Scenario = system_scenarios (kind='fault' for the symptom scenario; keyPosition/engineState/loadLevel fold into the NOW label). Provenance = PROVENANCE_LABEL map already in topology-detail-panel.tsx. labelGap + electricalContract feed the see-source expansion. branch_logic is NOT used on this screen (no fork yet ‚Äî confirm-complaint precedes the first branch). The symptom‚Üíhero-pin link is the only thing the schema can't do cleanly today (see gaps).
gaps: No symptom‚Üíhero-pin link. There is no column tying symptom (P0087) to THE pin whose reading defines the complaint (FRP Sensor ¬∑ Signal). Today it's resolvable only by name heuristic. MINIMAL PROPOSE: symptoms.symptomPinId:uuid (nullable FK component_pins) ‚Äî one column; OR derive it from the highest symptom_test_implications.priority test_action's componentId+pin (zero new columns, reuses existing ordering data ‚Äî preferred).

No machine-readable 'this reading is the fault' flag. pin_scenario_readings.reading is free prose ('...this is what the fault is showing'); scenario.kind='fault' is per-scenario not per-(pin,scenario). To light RED honestly at entry without parsing prose: MINIMAL PROPOSE pin_scenario_readings.isOutOfRange:boolean (nullable, curator-authored), set true ONLY for the fault scenario √ó the symptom pin. If absent, the screen must NOT fabricate red ‚Äî see incomplete state.

The four structured EXPECT columns (expectedValue/expectedUnit/expectedTolerance/meterMode) exist on test_actions but the loader SELECT drops all four. Surfacing them is a loader-select change Brandon must approve (do not apply); without them the EXPECT plate falls back to pin.expectedReading prose, which has no parsed band so the gauge degrades to a text band.

The exact FRP voltage-to-pressure curve is explicitly 'not yet captured' (pin.labelGap). So a true analog gauge with a needle position is not yet drawable; the EXPECT/NOW render as a labeled band + prose, not a precise dial ‚Äî and the see-source must say so.

(2) If the isOutOfRange flag (or fault-scenario‚Üîpin pairing) is absent, the verdict chip does NOT show red ‚Äî it shows neutral graphite "reported low" sourced verbatim from the reading prose, with the see-source explaining "this is the customer/scan-tool report, not a number we measured." Never a fake precise psi/volt figure. (4) All 25 parts are TRAINING-CONFIRMED ‚Üí uniform graphite "from theory" ‚Äî correct steady state, NOT an alarm; no amber unless a real labelGap/electricalContract note is surfaced in the see-source.

perShape.sceneExample: 2018 F-250 6.7L, P0087, intake (no prior tests). The tech opens the session. Ribbon: "‚Äπ sessions ¬∑ 2018 Ford F-250 ¬∑ 6.7L Power Stroke ¬∑ P0087 rail pressure low". dataModel.shapeSelector: Code picks the shape with a single deterministic resolver keyed on data already loaded plus the four dropped columns. STEP 1 (parent shape) = test_actions.observationMethod (loaded today at load-system-topology.ts:303). The 9-enum maps directly: scan_tool_pid -> single-value PID gauge; pressure_test_with_gauge -> fluid gauge; electrical_measurement_at_pin -> electrical-circuit canvas; waveform_capture -> scope/trace; direct_visual_internal | direct_visual_external | audible | touch | smell -> look/inspect good-vs-bad compare. STEP 2 (electrical SUB-shape: volts / drop / ohms / duty / Hz / amps / waveform) is decided by test_actions.meterMode (column meter_mode EXISTS at schema.ts:886, loader DROPS it ‚Äî surface it). Convention to read meterMode: 'vdc'|'volts'|'volts-dc' -> volts plate; 'volts-drop'|'vdrop' -> drop (across-two); 'ohms'|'continuity'|'resistance' -> continuity binary; 'duty'|'%'|'Hz' -> duty/ring; 'amps'|'A' -> amps (clamp); observationMethod=='waveform_capture' ALWAYS -> scope, regardless of meterMode. FALLBACK while meterMode is null: component_pins.roleAbbreviation (loaded) ‚Äî '12V'/'5V'/'SIG' -> volts, 'GND' -> ohms/continuity, 'PWM' -> duty. If both are absent, default to the volts value-plate (safest generic electrical readout) with verdict NEUTRAL. NOTHING NEW is required for shape selection: observationMethod is already loaded; meterMode only needs un-dropping. The ONLY genuinely new shape that the 9-enum cannot express is LOCATE/ORIENT (no enum value means 'find, do not measure') ‚Äî that needs the one new field below (test_actions.stepKind). HOOKUP is derived, not stored: meterMode=='volts-drop' -> across-two bracket; meterMode=='amps' -> clamp ring; observationMethod=='waveform_capture' -> scope lead; else one-lead-to-ground.

newFieldsProposed.name: stepKind
newFieldsProposed.why: The 9-value observationMethod enum describes a measurement or sensory test; it has NO way to say 'find the part first, suppress the reading' (LOCATE) or 'name the circuit, elevate no pin' (ORIENT). Today the prototype hard-codes type:'locate' precisely because no column exists. This is the single field that cannot be derived from existing data. Nullable: when null, shape falls back to observationMethod as today (zero regression).

newFieldsProposed.name: isOutOfRange
newFieldsProposed.why: Resolves direction risk #1. pin_scenario_readings.reading is free prose ('12V briefly during prime, then 0V') and pin.expectedReading is prose, so red + the failure-tree unfold have nothing structured per-(pin,scenario) to stand on. A nullable curator-authored boolean lets the verdict be true red only when explicitly marked ‚Äî set true ONLY for fault-scenario x symptom-pin pairs. CRITICAL: nullable so the absence -> NEUTRAL (never red), honoring 'never color a reading wrong we cannot prove wrong'. Preferred over re-parsing prose. Marked nice-to-have-leaning-must because the numeric path (expectedValue+expectedTolerance vs a captured number) covers operation scenarios once the 4 columns are surfaced; this boolean is the clean handle for fault scenarios and prose-only readings.

newFieldsProposed.name: symptomPinId
newFieldsProposed.why: The confirm-complaint entry screen needs to know WHICH pin's reading is the hero (the FRP signal pin that defines P0087). Today it is findable only by name heuristic. NICE-TO-HAVE because it is derivable for free: pick the highest symptom_test_implications.priority test_action and use its componentId/pin ‚Äî no new column needed if Brandon prefers derivation. Listed only so the choice is explicit.

dataModel.forkModel: A fork is NOT a new observationMethod shape ‚Äî it is the aftermath of any probe/pressure/look step once its reading lands, driven entirely by branch_logic. Selection: the just-completed test_action has branch_logic rows AND the NOW reading resolved one row to a verdict. The matched branch_logic.verdict (enum ok|warn|fail|impossible, loaded) drives the verdict chip + three-color discipline (graphite ok/warn, red ONLY on fail, impossible reads graphite 'reading shouldn't happen ‚Äî recheck hookup'). The loud consequence line is branch_logic.nextAction (loaded). The single load-bearing gap is branch_logic.routesToTestActionId (schema.ts:926, DROPPED): it points to the next test_actions.id -> testActions.componentId (+ its component_pins) = the ONE next part that lights and the directed leader's destination. branch_logic.reasoning (DROPPED) is the dimmed 'why this road?' pull. The unpicked road is NEVER drawn (honors no-step-N-of-M, no upcoming-work preview). No step table: order of which test ran is symptom_test_implications.priority; the fork just resolves which branch_logic row fired. Honest degrade: if routesToTestActionId is null/unsurfaced, show verdict + prose nextAction but auto-light NOTHING (words-only route); if no condition matches the prose reading, stay NEUTRAL (verdict undetermined), no red, tree collapsed.
dataModel.verdictAndRangeModel: Direction risk #1 resolved with a strict three-tier precedence and a NEUTRAL-when-ambiguous default ‚Äî code NEVER colors a reading wrong it cannot prove wrong. TIER 1 (preferred, numeric): once test_actions.expectedValue + expectedTolerance + expectedUnit are surfaced, AND a structured measured number exists for the (pin,scenario), compute in-range = |measured - expectedValue| <= expectedTolerance. Today no structured measured number is loaded (pin_scenario_readings.reading is prose; tech_outcomes.measured_value exists as a migration table but is NOT loaded / NOT in Drizzle defs). So Tier 1 is dormant for operation scenarios until either a captured number lands in a loaded tech_outcomes or the reading prose carries a parseable number ‚Äî DO NOT prose-parse to fabricate a verdict. TIER 2 (the clean structured handle): the proposed nullable pin_scenario_readings.isOutOfRange boolean, curator-authored, set true ONLY for fault-scenario x symptom-pin pairs (e.g. FRP signal pin x fault-low). When present it is authoritative: true -> red verdict + unfold pin.missingLogic in place; false -> neutral/in-range, tree stays collapsed. TIER 3 (fork verdict): on a resolved fork, branch_logic.verdict==='fail' lights red and unfolds the tree; ok/warn stay graphite. DEFAULT (the spine rule): when none of the three resolve ‚Äî expectedValue/tolerance unauthored AND isOutOfRange null AND no matched fail branch ‚Äî the verdict chip is NEUTRAL graphite ('reading' / 'reported low' verbatim from prose), NO red, NO green, and the failure tree stays COLLAPSED. The failure-tree-unfold trigger is exactly 'verdict resolved to fail/out-of-range' ‚Äî it is gated, never pre-loaded onto an in-range or neutral reading. The one screen that lights red at entry without a fresh probe is confirm-complaint, and only when isOutOfRange (or the fault-scenario x symptom-pin pairing) actually marks the reading ‚Äî otherwise it shows neutral 'reported low' sourced from prose.
dataModel.orderingModel: No new step/walk/sequence table ‚Äî a 'step' IS a test_action. The ORDER of steps is symptom_test_implications.priority (integer 1-10, higher first; loaded today, indexed at symptom_test_implications_symptom_priority_idx). symptom_test_implications.testActionId already drives implicatedByCurrentSymptom (loader lines 334-346) ‚Äî the same rows, read with their priority, give the ordered walk. WHICH step is current = SelectionState in components/screens/topology-diagnostic.tsx (activeStep / focusPinId) walking that priority-sorted list; index 0 before any commit = the confirm-complaint entry shape. The FORK graph (branch_logic.routesToTestActionId) overrides linear priority order when a reading routes the tech to a specific next test_action. So: priority gives the default silent ordering; the branch_logic graph reroutes on a resolved verdict. The system computes 'next' silently from priority + branch graph and shows only done + now ‚Äî no step N of M, no upcoming-work preview, honoring the cognitive-load rule. No schema change for ordering.
dataModel.hookupModel: The meter-hookup overlay (where lead-dots / clamp / bracket / scope-lead / gauge-tee sit) is DERIVED, not stored. Endpoints come from existing data: the lit pin = component_pins (the focus pin, with edge/displayOrder/roleAbbreviation, loaded); the ground reference = the sibling pin where roleAbbreviation='GND' / the component_connections row with electricalRole='ground'; the directed path = component_connections with electricalRole non-null (loaded; electrical) or connectionKind='fluid-line' (loaded; fluid). Hookup FORM is derived from meterMode + observationMethod: one-lead-to-ground (default electrical), across-two/bracket (meterMode='volts-drop'), clamp ring (meterMode='amps'), scope lead+clip (observationMethod='waveform_capture'), gauge-tee on the fluid edge (observationMethod='pressure_test_with_gauge'), no overlay at all on LOCATE/ORIENT (suppressed) and on a fork (post-measurement). WHAT IS MISSING for true two-point / span hookups: a test_action measures across a SINGLE componentId today, and component_pins hang singly off a component ‚Äî there is no way to say 'measure across pump-GND pin to the chassis stud' for voltage-drop, and the chassis-ground stud is not always a modeled node. The existing partial answer: component_connections.fromPinId/toPinId (loaded, currently mostly null) CAN carry the span endpoints ‚Äî surface/author them rather than add columns. For voltage-drop specifically, if curators cannot express the span via fromPinId/toPinId on a connection, the minimal fallback is to render one probe mark + the description string and put a quiet 'second probe point not yet captured' in the see-source (amber). A clamp/scope hookup needs nothing new (single pin + derived form). RECOMMENDATION: prefer authoring component_connections.fromPinId/toPinId for spans over adding from_pin_id/to_pin_id to test_actions ‚Äî the connection row already models the conductor being dropped across.

dataModel.migrationSketch: Two of the three new fields need a migration; the column-un-dropping needs NONE (meterMode/expectedValue/expectedUnit/expectedTolerance/routesToTestActionId/reasoning/test_actions.sourceProvenance all already exist in the DB per schema.ts ‚Äî purely a loader-SELECT change). dataModel.scalesWithZeroPerCaseDesign: With this model the finished screen renders itself from vetted rows. Add a new MAKE/SYSTEM: seed components + component_connections + component_pins + system_scenarios + pin_scenario_readings for it; the canvas auto-draws the directed path (electricalRole / connectionKind color the edges) and the marks (role-colored point + serif name), zero layout code per case. Add a new SYMPTOM: seed the symptom row + symptom_test_implications (priority) linking it to existing test_actions; the walk ORDER and the implicated-test highlighting compute themselves; the entry confirm-complaint screen picks its hero from the highest-priority implication. Add a new STEP: seed a test_action ‚Äî its observationMethod auto-selects the parent shape, its meterMode the electrical sub-shape, its expectedValue/Unit/Tolerance fill the EXPECT plate/gauge, its branch_logic rows (with routesToTestActionId) wire the fork; the per-step-TYPE fact budget (PROBE leads with probeLocation; LOCATE suppresses the reading via stepKind; ORIENT elevates no pin; failure tree stays collapsed until isOutOfRange/verdict=fail) is decided by the shape resolver, not by hand. The three-color provenance (from-theory graphite default / needs-field-check amber from labelGap+electricalContract / measured-wrong red gated on verdict) is data-driven from sourceProvenance + labelGap. Nothing on the screen is positioned or branched per individual case ‚Äî every visual is a function of (observationMethod, meterMode, stepKind, the four EXPECT columns, branch_logic, the scenario maps, provenance). That is the scaling bet: drop in vetted data, the shape renders, ZERO per-case design.
