visualSpec.marks: A part is NEVER a rectangle/card ‚Äî delete the .topo-node faceplate entirely. A mark = one role-colored POINT (9px disc, 1.5px ring) + a one-line Instrument-Serif name (--vt-font-serif, ~15px, letter-spacing -0.01em). kind/location/probe text are SUPPRESSED on canvas (they live in the Meter); set .node .kd,.node .loc{display:none}. Point color = the part's dominant electrical role token if it carries pins (e.g. a 12V-fed pump rings warm-red --role-12v), else --vt-elem-* by component.kind (sensor‚Üí--vt-elem-oxygen, valve/actuator‚Üí--vt-elem-vacuum, pump‚Üí--vt-elem-fuel, module‚Üí--vt-bone-600). THREE TIERS drive a CLASS, never display:none (fade-not-vanish): focus = full ink, name --vt-fg, point ring --vt-bone-700; anchor = opacity 0.45, the single "why we're here" symptom part (FRP Sensor) with a mono micro-cap symptom-flag on a --vt-bg knockout, point ring dashed --vt-bone-500; recede = opacity var(--vt-recede) (0.16), pointer-events:none, label opacity 0.10, frozen. ACTIVE (the part under test) = navy halo: point background color-mix(--vt-signal-500 14%, --vt-bone-50), ring --vt-signal-500, box-shadow 0 0 0 3px var(--vt-bg),0 0 0 4.5px color-mix(--vt-signal-500 30%,transparent); name goes italic --vt-fg. THE 1.4px BORDER (topology.css:91) IS REPLACED by a 0.5px engraved hairline ONLY where a hairline is still wanted (selection tint = --vt-accent-bg fill + 0.5px --vt-rule, never the old 2px amber border which collides with amber=caution). PROVENANCE MODULATES THE DRAW (no chip): TRAINING-CONFIRMED/INFERRED ‚Üí solid point ring (default); FIELD-VERIFIED ‚Üí solid ring + a single --vt-signal-500 navy tick on the name baseline; GAP ‚Üí hairline-DASHED point ring (the universal schematic "provisional" convention) ‚Äî that dashed edge, not a standing chip, IS the gap signal. Radii small (--vt-radius-1..2): instruments don't have pillows.
visualSpec.wireVocab: Directed lines with meaning, never decorative. STROKE COLOR by component_connections.electricalRole via the six newly-defined --role-* tokens: signal‚Üí--role-signal, 5v-ref‚Üí--role-5v-ref, low-ref‚Üí--role-low-ref, pwm‚Üí--role-pwm, 12v‚Üí--role-12v (warm red, power=hot), ground‚Üí--role-ground (graphite, return=neutral). FLUID lines (connectionKind='fluid-line', electricalRole null) stroke --role-fuel; mechanical/can-bus/lin-bus ‚Üí --vt-bone-500 hairline. DIRECTION comes from connection.direction + from‚Üíto ordering; draw as clean elbowed cubic paths (M..C with a mid-x control), arrowing implied by flow not arrowheads. CURRENT/FLOW ANIMATION is tied to scenario_wire_states.wireState and gated to MEANING: a wire animates the dash-flow (stroke-dasharray 5 6, @keyframes flow to{stroke-dashoffset:-22}, ~1.1s linear) ONLY when (a) it is the active wire of a PROBE step AND (b) its wireState is a live-current class (steady-12v / signal-* / pwm-*). pwm-low‚Üípwm-max modulate the flow SPEED/pulse-width so duty rises visibly with load before any number is read; steady-gnd / off / a LOCATE step / a fork (post-measurement) ‚Üí NO animation (movement would imply a measurement that isn't happening). ACTIVE wire = reuse the existing .wire.is-active (stroke-width 3.5 + drop-shadow(0 0 6px currentColor)). RECEDED wires drop to opacity var(--vt-recede) (0.16) and freeze (animation:none) ‚Äî they stay DRAWN (the brief's fade-not-vanish), returning to full only in whole-system; .wire.dim (0.25) is the in-circuit-but-not-active leg. HAIRLINE LEADER from pin to Meter: a 1px dashed (2 3) --vt-signal-500 line at opacity 0.5 runs from the active pin chip up to the Meter card's top edge so the reading is provably anchored to WHERE it comes from; suppressed on LOCATE/ORIENT/fork (no live reading to anchor).
visualSpec.meterHookupOverlay: The overlay is DERIVED from (meterMode + observationMethod), not stored ‚Äî it draws the leads exactly where they physically connect, doubling as the WHERE proof. ONE-LEAD-TO-GROUND (default electrical, meterMode volts/null): a bright navy probe-glyph clipped on the active pin + a faint reference lead-glyph on the ground node/symbol ‚Äî two leads of a DC reading drawn where they go. ACROSS-TWO-POINTS (meterMode='volts-drop'/'vdrop'): TWO bright lead-glyphs, one at each end of the SAME conductor segment, joined by a faint navy bracket arc that lifts into the Meter ‚Äî reads "measuring the difference across this," never one-to-ground; endpoints come from component_connections.fromPinId/toPinId (author them, don't add test_actions columns). CLAMP-AROUND-WIRE (meterMode='amps'/'A'): a thin navy open clamp-RING glyph encircling the wire (‚â•20px so it survives mobile) with a short stub to a scope corner ‚Äî current read by induction, the only non-contact hookup, never a dot on a pin. OHMS/CONTINUITY (meterMode ohms/continuity): two lead-glyphs across A‚ÜîB with the source de-energized ‚Äî wire drawn COLD (no flow-dash, key-off look) + a small Œ© glyph at the clamp; the chassis-ground endpoint B, when unmodeled, renders as a labeled earth glyph ("chassis stud ¬∑ location not captured") not an invented node. SCOPE (observationMethod='waveform_capture'): a single scope-probe glyph on the pin + a ground-CLIP glyph on the nearest ground, leader rising to the trace window. FLUID GAUGE-TEE (observationMethod='pressure_test_with_gauge'): a small circle-with-needle gauge glyph TEED onto the fluid edge at the tap point with a short branch stub. NO OVERLAY on LOCATE/ORIENT (suppressed ‚Äî the slot shows a LOOK/FIND verb marker instead) and NO overlay on a fork (post-measurement; leads are off the part ‚Äî the overlay returns on the next routed-to screen).
visualSpec.gauge: The instrument block is mono tnum throughout (--vt-font-mono + font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums) so digits never jitter on scenario flip ‚Äî this single discipline is most of the gauge-vs-webtext feel. THREE COLUMNS, grid auto/1fr/auto. EXPECT = a quiet expected-band FRAME (0.5px --vt-bone-600 border, --vt-radius-4, --vt-bone-50 fill, inset 0 1px 0 oklch(100% 0 0/.6)): the fat value .vt-mono-big (~46px desktop, weight 600, letter-spacing -0.03em) composed from test_actions.expectedValue + a .unit span (~24px) for expectedUnit, with a "¬±<expectedTolerance> <unit>" sub-line when tolerance is present; an --vt-fg-3 mono expect-note carries expectedObservation/pin.expectedReading prose. NOW = self-labels the scenario in its column header ("NOW ¬∑ Key On ¬∑ Engine Off") and shows the live pin_scenario_readings prose (~20px mono), settling like a damped needle on scenario flip; provenance caption sits directly beneath. VERDICT = ONE chip tied to the reading, never a decorative pill: in-range "‚óê in range" (--vt-signal-600 ink, color-mix navy 45% border, navy-5% tint), out-of-range red (--vt-risk-high border/ink for low/check, --vt-risk-destructive for hard fault), neutral "‚óê reading" (--vt-fg-3, no color) when nothing structured can judge it. THE THREE-COLOR DISCIPLINE IS ABSOLUTE: graphite = drafted/in-range default (the resting state, not an alarm), amber (--vt-amber-500 elevated / --vt-amber-600 ink) = needs-field-check, elevated ONLY at the moment of a probe and never standing on a faded node, red = measured-wrong ONLY. EXPECT BAND determinacy follows the verdict precedence: Tier1 numeric (|measured‚àíexpectedValue|‚â§expectedTolerance once the 4 columns are surfaced AND a structured measured number exists), Tier2 the curator isOutOfRange boolean (authoritative when present), Tier3 a resolved branch_logic.verdict='fail'; DEFAULT = NEUTRAL graphite, NO red/green, failure tree COLLAPSED ‚Äî code never colors a reading wrong it cannot prove wrong. The failure tree (pin.missingLogic) unfolds in place ONLY on a resolved fail/out-of-range, height+opacity over --vt-dur-3.
visualSpec.waveformTrace: For observationMethod='waveform_capture' the numeric gauge is REPLACED by a trace window (the gauge becomes a scope face) ‚Äî the only sub-shape whose hero is a curve, not a digit, per the PicoScope reference-fixed convention. LAYOUT: a thin-ruled scope graticule drawn as ENGRAVED hairlines on the bone surface (NEVER react-flow's dotted Background) holding TWO superimposed traces ‚Äî a FIXED known-good REFERENCE trace (quiet graphite ghost, --vt-recede weight, "EXPECT ¬∑ shape") under the LIVE captured trace in navy ink (--vt-signal-500, "NOW ¬∑ <scenario>"), so good-vs-bad reads by perception not memory. TRACE GEOMETRY is generated from scenario_wire_states.wireState (no waveform image needed, the enum IS the shape grammar): pwm-low|med|high|max ‚Üí a SQUARE wave whose mark:space duty climbs with load; signal-rest|low|med|high|pegged ‚Üí an ANALOG sweep whose amplitude rises (pegged = flat near rail); off|steady-* ‚Üí a flat line. Sub-quantity forks the renderer by roleAbbreviation: 'PWM' ‚Üí square, 'SIG' ‚Üí analog. A compact mono caption beside the trace gives the structured band when present (meterMode label e.g. "PWM duty %", expectedValue + expectedUnit + ¬±tolerance) so trace-shape and number agree; on scenario flip the reference stays FIXED and the live trace re-draws + settles ~280ms. Verdict = SHAPE-MATCH ("matches reference" navy / "wrong shape" red), not digit-vs-digit; missingLogic unfolds only on an off-shape capture. Timebase is DERIVED (meterMode+expectedValue ‚Üí show ~4 cycles), no new column. Two-pin compare is native: tapping the complementary pin (B) docks its trace as a second navy overlay in the same window, capped at two so it stays a scope not a table. INCOMPLETE STATE: when no referenceWaveform is authored (today's reality), draw the live trace from wireState honestly styled "from theory," render the reference as a faint dashed generic-shape ghost labeled "shape from theory ¬∑ amplitude/frequency not yet captured" (amber), and SUPPRESS the verdict to neutral ‚Äî never a fabricated 50%/100Hz.
visualSpec.forkUI: A fork is NOT a new observationMethod shape ‚Äî it is the AFTERMATH of any probe/pressure/look step once branch_logic resolves a verdict; selected when the just-completed test_action has branch_logic rows AND the NOW reading matched one row's condition. NO decision-diamond, NO flowchart, NO branching tree, NO ghosted alternative, NO "or else", NO count. It is drawn as a BATON-PASS along the harness: (1) the just-cleared circuit recedes one notch toward --vt-recede and its name desaturates to --vt-fg-3; (2) the ONE next part the matched branch_logic.routesToTestActionId points at BRIGHTENS to focus ink, gains the navy .node.active halo, and slides toward center; (3) a single animated leader ‚Äî reuse .wire.is-active (3.5px + glow) ‚Äî sweeps from the cleared pin to the next part over --vt-dur-3 via a stroke-dashoffset draw-on, colored by the NEXT leg's role (--role-12v warm-red if routing upstream to relay/fuse power, --role-fuel muted-green if routing along supply to the HP side). THE UNPICKED ROAD IS NEVER DRAWN. The Meter format becomes VERDICT‚ÜíROUTE (no fresh EXPECT band ‚Äî nothing new is measured): line 1 = the carried-forward reading gone GREY (settled history, no expected-band frame) + the resolved verdict chip (graphite for ok/warn, red ONLY on fail; impossible reads graphite "reading shouldn't happen ‚Äî recheck hookup"); line 2 (the loud hero, serif) = branch_logic.nextAction ("Move to the high-pressure side ‚Äî CP4.2 and the rail regulator"). One tap down, dimmed: branch_logic.reasoning as the "why this road?" see-source pull. HONORS no-step-N-of-M / no-upcoming-work-preview (the system computes next silently from symptom_test_implications.priority overridden by the branch graph; shows done + now only). DEGRADE: routesToTestActionId null/unsurfaced ‚Üí show verdict + prose nextAction but auto-light NOTHING (words-only route, "next part isn't wired in yet"); no condition matches ‚Üí NEUTRAL, no fork, no red, tree collapsed ("reading logged ‚Äî verdict undetermined").
visualSpec.locateAndLook: TWO reading-suppressed shapes that prove the per-step-TYPE budget. LOCATE/ORIENT (find the part, hide the number): no real observationMethod expresses it ‚Äî selected by the proposed nullable test_actions.stepKind ('locate'|'orient'), falling back to the prototype's hard-coded type:'locate' until the column lands. The EXPECT/NOW/verdict block is ENTIRELY SUPPRESSED (m-instr display:none) ‚Äî a number is noise when the job is "find the thing." The Meter still rises but leads with the location string as the HERO (serif italic, since there is no mono reading to be hero): eyebrow "FIND" (not WHERE/PROBE), then component_pins.probeLocation or components.location ("Find the pump's ground stud ‚Äî driver-side frame rail, the chassis stud near the tank"). NO meter-hookup overlay, NO leader line, NO failure tree (a tree presumes a measurement that can be wrong); the active wire does NOT animate. ORIENT elevates no pin and shows no reading ‚Äî it just names + frames the circuit. Below fold: location detail, the amber needs-field-check (real labelGap only), Operational Theori. Provenance still shows "from theory ¬∑ why?" since the location claim is drafted. LOOK/INSPECT (good-vs-bad compare, no number): observationMethod ‚àà {direct_visual_internal, direct_visual_external, audible, touch, smell} ‚Äî these rows have expectedValue/Unit/Tolerance/meterMode all NULL, and that NULL IS the signal there is no gauge. The gauge slot is REPLACED by a good-vs-bad COMPARE: two facing hairline-framed swatches (0.5px frame, serif text inside) ‚Äî GOOD (left, from expectedObservation) vs FAULT (right, from branch_logic.condition where verdict='fail'), the verdict reading "which one are you looking at?" not "what number." The hookup slot holds a mono micro-cap inspection verb LOOK/LISTEN/FEEL/SMELL (never an emoji) where the leads label would sit. Sub-layout forks: visual ‚Üí swatch pair; audible ‚Üí a quiet signal-rest line glyph (NOT a scope trace) beside the sound words; touch/smell ‚Üí purely typographic. The verdict is a human tap target (looks-good / fault-seen, written to tech_outcomes when wired, else transient); the FAULT swatch stays graphite until "fault seen" flips it red and unfolds the ONE next move (branch nextAction). The part sits on its real fluid-flow segment with NO dash-flow animation (nothing is live current on a look step).
visualSpec.provenance: Provenance is INK, not a badge wall ‚Äî and it lives in ONE privileged place: the line directly beneath the EXPECT/NOW value, because that is the number the tech is about to act on. KILL the standing topo-node__gap "needs field check" chip at topology-node.tsx:65 ‚Äî provenance instead MODULATES THE DRAW: FIELD-VERIFIED part at full ink + a single navy --vt-signal-500 tick; TRAINING-CONFIRMED/INFERRED one shade lighter (the neutral default); GAP part drawn with a hairline-DASHED point ring (the schematic "provisional" convention). Confidence is felt before a word is read. The caption uses the existing PROVENANCE_LABEL map verbatim, three-color discipline: TRAINING-CONFIRMED ‚Üí "from theory" / TRAINING-INFERRED ‚Üí "inferred from theory" (neutral graphite --vt-fg-3, the default, NOT an alarm); FIELD-VERIFIED ‚Üí "field-verified" (quiet navy tick); GAP ‚Üí "needs field verification" (amber --vt-amber-600). RECONCILE THE WORDING: the shipped PROVENANCE_LABEL map renders GAP as "needs field verification" ‚Äî use THAT string in the caption to match the code, and reserve the shorter "needs field check" for the in-line secondary-fact LABEL only (the m-fact eyebrow), so the two phrasings have distinct, non-conflicting homes rather than drifting. INLINE "WHY?" PULL (pull, not push): the caption word itself is the affordance ‚Äî "from theory ¬∑ why?" / "needs field verification ¬∑ why?". Tapping it expands the rationale INLINE, in place (max-height+opacity over --vt-dur-3), pushing secondary facts down briefly, then folds away on a second tap or background-tap ‚Äî never a standing sources column, never navigation away. The expanded body is short and every-word-counts: the grade in plain terms ("Drafted from how this system works on the 6.7 Power Stroke ‚Äî not yet verified against a wiring diagram for this exact truck") + the specific labelGap/electricalContract unknowns ("Wire color, connector pin number, prime duration aren't captured yet"). AMBER is the ONE provenance state allowed elevated salience, and ONLY at the moment of a probe on the active reading ‚Äî never standing on a faded node (habituation guard). The labelGap text lives ONLY inside the expanded why-path, never on the canvas. RED is never provenance ‚Äî red is measured-wrong only.
visualSpec.scenarioChrome: No persistent scenario bar. The active scenario is NAMED INSIDE the instrument block's NOW column header ("NOW ¬∑ Key On ¬∑ Engine Off", derived from system_scenarios keyPosition/engineState/loadLevel for operation rows, or the slug/label for fault rows) so the live reading SELF-LABELS ‚Äî the tech never cross-references a separate strip to know which truck-state the number belongs to. The full picker (the Ignition/Engine/Load groups + pretend-fault, reusing scenario-bar.tsx deriveCompound/findOperationSlug ‚Äî note these are INTERNAL/not-exported, so either export them or rebuild the picker behind the chip; do not claim unchanged reuse) collapses into a single RECESSED RIBBON CHIP at the right of the orientation ribbon ("Key On ¬∑ Engine Off ‚ñæ", 0.5px --vt-rule-strong border, --vt-bone-50, mono micro-cap) that opens a compact scenario sheet on demand. The standalone "Now showing ¬∑" strip is DELETED on desktop (its job absorbed into the NOW column) and survives on mobile ONLY as a thin status line pinned just above the sheet. Scenario change feels like turning the key: flip the chip and the NOW value settles with a damped ~280ms tnum transition (the .m-now.flip opacity micro-move, no jitter ‚Äî tabular numerals), the EXPECT band stays FIXED as the reference, the anchor shifts faintly. For continuity/key-state-independent shapes the chip DIMS and carries an "any" hint, and the NOW header reads "NOW ¬∑ key-off (any key state)" ‚Äî the screen states once that key state doesn't move this reading rather than theatrically re-settling it. The curator captured/missing dataStatus (topology.dataStatus) is ROLE-GATED SERVER-SIDE behind a curator-only ribbon entry (never the bottom overlay it is today, never shown to techs/owners), opening a tiny clearly-labeled "Curator only ¬∑ coverage" panel.
visualSpec.mobile: 375‚Äì414px is a HARD GATE, same Meter grammar. RIBBON 44px: back + vehicle + code + ONE scenario chip ("Key On ‚ñæ", #scenChipLabel ellipsis-clamped to ~96px); the "whole system" and "curator" controls DROP (.r-whole/.r-curator display:none). The lit circuit AUTO-FRAMES into the TOP ~45‚Äì48% filling the width; for vertical paths the source‚Üípart‚Üíground stacks top-to-bottom so it reads on a narrow column; the receded harness holds behind at --vt-recede. Pin dots and every tap target ‚â•48px; provenance "from theory ¬∑ why?" and the secondary toggle are ‚â•44px. THE METER is a NON-MODAL bottom sheet with peek/half/full DETENTS (NO scrim at peek/half ‚Äî canvas stays LIVE above it, per Material/Apple largestUndimmedDetent), a grabber, 14px top radius. PEEK (~150px) leads with the hero: EXPECT plate is the LARGEST type on the phone (mono tnum ~38px) beside NOW, verdict chip wraps to its own row (.m-verdict grid-column 1/-1), .m-expect-note hidden. HALF reveals connector/wires/Operational Theori + the see-source; FULL adds the rest. CRITICAL COUPLING: as the sheet RISES the camera PANS the lit part UP (Turo technique) so the sheet NEVER covers the part under test ‚Äî the spine-forbidden failure ‚Äî coupled by an explicit sheet-height‚Üícamera-offset formula and tested AT 375px as an acceptance gate. Shape-specific: duty ring scales down but stays hero; waveform trace takes full sheet width at half detent (it benefits most from wide-but-short); LOCATE/CONFIRM are LOW-RISK for the pan coupling (no lit pin under a lead) ‚Äî confirm-complaint and locate are the safest of the family at 375px. A thin "Now ¬∑ Key On" mono status line pins just above the sheet (the only survivor of the desktop scenario strip). The desktop vt kit is desktop-only, so this collapsed-chip + bottom-sheet + ‚â•48px layer is genuinely NEW companion CSS and becomes the template for every future in-the-bay mobile surface.
visualSpec.motion: Calm, damped, no bounce ‚Äî every motion does diagnostic work or is cut (the OP-1 test), all on the kit's --vt-ease-instrument (cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1)); durations --vt-dur-1..4 (80/160/280/480ms). (1) STEP REFRAME ‚Äî a gentle fitView settle over --vt-dur-4 (480ms) interpolate:'smooth', maxZoom ~1.1 (a settle, not The Drive's flight), run TOGETHER with the receded nodes/wires opacity cross-fade to --vt-recede over --vt-dur-3 so the eye reads ONE settle; the reading rising is the louder beat. (2) THE METER RISE ‚Äî translateY(105%‚Üí0) + opacity over --vt-dur-3 ease-out, no overshoot; dismiss reverses it and the canvas reclaims full space (the dismiss-returns-full-canvas behavior is a HARD requirement, since the persistent bottom home is what flirts with the docked-panel line). (3) VALUE SETTLE on scenario flip ‚Äî the tnum digits settle (opacity/position micro-move over --vt-dur-2, 160ms), never snap, like a damped analog needle; EXPECT stays fixed. (4) FOCUS-ON-TAP commit ‚Äî start on pointerdown (all data in-memory, zero round-trip), a single decisive navy halo + ~1.02 scale over --vt-dur-1 (80ms), Things-3-confident not web-hover-soft. (5) FAILURE-TREE UNFOLD ‚Äî height+opacity reveal in place over --vt-dur-3, ONLY on a resolved out-of-range. (6) FORK baton-pass ‚Äî the leader draws on via stroke-dashoffset over --vt-dur-3 as the old circuit recedes in the same beat. REDUCED-MOTION (hard, first-class branch): under prefers-reduced-motion, set all fitView durations to 0 (instant frame ‚Äî the nausea-prone move dropped), keep ONLY opacity cross-fades, FREEZE wire dash-flow (the existing @media guard already does this for .wire), render waveform as a static representative cycle, snap duty digits ‚Äî the destination framing and the reading are IDENTICAL, proving the felt value lives in the information design, not the animation.
visualSpec.wholeSystemEscape: Explicit, but NOT the default ‚Äî the default is the narrowed per-step circuit (just the few elements the step involves), never the system map with one part highlighted. The ONE exception that earns the full end-to-end map is the confirm-complaint ENTRY screen (the only screen with no step advanced): it draws the whole fuel spine at uniform quiet graphite with exactly ONE part elevated (the FRP Sensor, the symptom signal), because the complaint is "the number this sensor reports is wrong" ‚Äî and even there no circuit is narrowed (narrowing is the next screen's job). Everywhere else the whole system is an opt-in ESCAPE HATCH: a custom minimal "‚åñ whole system" control (replacing react-flow's default <Controls/>) in the desktop ribbon that calls fitView() with NO nodes filter, bringing every node to a readable mid-opacity (~0.52) and RETURNING the full harness wiring that is .wire.off while focused (the crossing-lines web is noise during a step, context only in the overview). It closes the Meter, drops the leader, and any node tap / "back to step" returns to the narrowed view. On MOBILE the whole-system control DROPS from the 44px ribbon (screen real estate); the escape is reached via the curator/overflow path, keeping the phone on the lit circuit. This honors the core principle: the screen IS the circuit you are testing, composed automatically from whatever the current step needs ‚Äî the whole truck is one deliberate tap away, never the resting state.

adversarial.lens: Coverage completeness ‚Äî every step shape AND every Tier-1/Tier-2 electrical sub-shape must have BOTH a defined screen AND a defined honest "data incomplete" state; Tier-3/4 must degrade, never blank. Verified against real loader (lib/diagnostics/load-system-topology.ts), schema (lib/db/schema.ts), tokens (app/globals.css, components/topology/topology.css), the scene-data slice, seed dir, and migration 0021.

airGaps.where: Waveform/Scope family (4 shapes: electrical.waveform.scope, electrical.waveform.current-clamp-scope, electrical/duty-pwm, and the standalone 'waveform' shape) ‚Äî ALL select on observationMethod==='waveform_capture'. VERIFIED: waveform_capture appears ZERO times in any repo-resident data (seed dir holds only a 963-byte calibration-seed.ts; migration 0021 is DDL-only; grep across *.sql/*.ts/*.json finds it ONLY as a schema enum value and in the synthesis prompt scaffold). The actual P0087 test_actions live only in the live Supabase DB, uninspectable here. So four shapes' PARENT TRIGGER cannot be confirmed to fire on this scene's data at all. Worse, the plan is internally contradictory: electrical/duty-pwm says PWM pins fire on waveform_capture, while electrical-tier34-graceful lists PWM under BOTH electrical_measurement_at_pin AND waveform_capture. If the real PWM test_actions carry electrical_measurement_at_pin (the more likely value for a back-probe duty read ‚Äî and the only electrical method present in repo data, x2), then the duty-PWM HERO is the value-plate/duty-ring shape, NOT the scope-trace shape, and the entire trace-window Meter mode is never reached for this scene.
airGaps.fix: Before building, query the live DB for the actual observation_method on the vcv-a/vcv-b/reg-a/reg-b/frp-signal test_actions (SELECT slug, observation_method, meter_mode FROM test_actions JOIN component_pins...). Then make the shape resolver's PWM branch deterministic on the REAL value found, and pick ONE home for duty-PWM (trace-window vs value/ring) rather than both. Document the answer in the plan so the trace-window code path isn't built for a trigger that never fires on the seeded scene.

airGaps.where: Tier-3/4 sub-shapes named with NO rendering primitive: AC charging ripple (sine glyph), diode test (forward-drop band + OL), min/max capture (two stacked tnum cells), Hz/frequency, bidirectional active-command. The tier34-graceful shape's screenContents/meterBlock NAME these but the VISUAL SPEC (marks/wireVocab/meterHookupOverlay/gauge/waveformTrace) defines drawing primitives ONLY for: one-lead-to-ground, across-two bracket, clamp ring, ohms/cold-wire, scope, gauge-tee, square-wave, analog-sweep. There is NO defined glyph for a sine/ripple trace, NO defined two-cell min|max gauge layout, NO defined diode forward/reverse dual-readout. The plan asserts these 'degrade gracefully' but the degrade target (qualitative band + 'capture to compare') is the SAME for all of them ‚Äî meaning the tool literally cannot draw the distinguishing mark for ripple vs diode vs min/max; they all collapse to one indistinct skeleton.
airGaps.fix: Either (a) explicitly scope AC-ripple/diode/min-max/Hz OUT of V2 (the scene has no charging/diode component, so they're never triggered ‚Äî say so and stop claiming a screen for them), or (b) add the three missing rendering primitives to the visual spec (sine glyph, two-cell min|max, dual forward/reverse readout). Do not leave them named-but-undrawable; that is the textbook air gap ‚Äî a place the tool would not know what to draw.

airGaps.where: voltage-drop two-point span hookup. The shape REQUIRES two probe endpoints across one conductor, but the plan's own data model admits a test_action has a single componentId and pins hang singly, and component_connections.fromPinId/toPinId are 'loaded, currently mostly null'. So on real data the across-two bracket overlay has no second endpoint to anchor. The incomplete-state IS defined (one probe mark + 'second probe point not yet captured' amber), but the FULL (non-degraded) screen is undrawable on ANY current data ‚Äî there is no authored span in the scene. This means the voltage-drop shape ships as permanently-degraded with no path to its hero state until curators author fromPinId/toPinId, which no tooling or task in the plan provisions.
airGaps.fix: Confirm whether ANY component_connection in the live scene has non-null fromPinId+toPinId. If none, the voltage-drop hero screen has zero exercising data ‚Äî either author one span row for the lp-gnd‚Üíchassis conductor as a proof case, or mark voltage-drop as degraded-only for V2. Add a curator authoring task for fromPinId/toPinId to the plan; today it is assumed but unprovisioned.

airGaps.where: Continuity/resistance endpoint B (chassis ground stud). The resistance shape's A‚ÜîB span needs B as a node, but the plan confirms 'chassis ground is NOT a node in the 25 components.' The incomplete-state (synthetic earth glyph from a ground-role connection with toPinId=null) IS defined ‚Äî BUT it depends on a component_connection row with electricalRole='ground' AND toPinId=null existing. The scene-data slice shows the relay‚Üípump 12v edge but the ground-to-chassis edge is unverified (only 5 electrical-wire connections total exist; the plan itself flags 'confirm the seed actually models the chassis stud as a node/edge'). If that ground edge is absent, the resistance shape draws a lit pin with NO span and NO synthetic glyph ‚Äî a blank where the hero A‚ÜîB should be.
airGaps.fix: Verify in the live DB that an electrical-wire connection with electricalRole='ground' exists from the lift pump (toPinId or toComponentId resolving to chassis). If absent, the synthetic-earth-glyph fallback has nothing to key off and the screen blanks the span. Make the renderer draw the earth glyph from observationMethod+ohms-meterMode ALONE (not requiring the connection row) so the incomplete-state is truly never-blank.

airGaps.where: LOCATE/ORIENT shape selection on real data. The shape is selected by the PROPOSED test_actions.stepKind column, which does NOT exist yet (migration is sketched, not applied). The fallback selector ('observationMethod=direct_visual_external + invasiveness=1 + prose-sniff of expectedObservation') is admitted unreliable. The prototype hard-codes type:'locate'. So TODAY there is NO reliable way to auto-pick LOCATE ‚Äî meaning the 'find the lift pump ground stud' canonical step cannot be rendered as a LOCATE screen from data; it would render as whatever observationMethod its test_action carries (likely electrical_measurement_at_pin ‚Üí the resistance PROBE shape, showing the reading band the LOCATE shape is supposed to SUPPRESS).
airGaps.fix: Until stepKind lands, the LOCATE shape has no data-driven trigger and the reading-suppression cannot fire ‚Äî the per-step-type budget thesis (LOCATE suppresses the number) is unprovable for V2. Either gate the LOCATE shape behind the stepKind migration as a hard prerequisite (not 'nice-to-have'), or define a deterministic interim trigger that real test_actions satisfy. airGaps.where: confirm-complaint RED-at-entry verdict. The shape lights red on entry from the fault-low FRP reading. The data model's verdict precedence puts this on Tier-2 (isOutOfRange boolean ‚Äî a PROPOSED, non-existent column) or 'fault-scenario x symptom-pin pairing.' The symptom‚Üípin link is ALSO proposed/derived (symptomPinId nullable, or derive from priority). The incomplete-state (degrade to neutral 'reported low') IS defined and honest, but it means the FLAGSHIP entry screen ships in its degraded form (neutral, no red) until two new columns land ‚Äî the 'complaint already failed, red lit' hero is unreachable from current data.
airGaps.fix: Acceptable as honest degrade, but state plainly in the plan that confirm-complaint ships NEUTRAL (not red) for V2 until isOutOfRange + symptomPinId exist. Don't present the red-at-entry as the V2 deliverable when its two structural prerequisites are unbuilt.

airGaps.where: fork-decision next-part lighting. routesToTestActionId is dropped by the loader (VERIFIED: branchRows SELECT at lines 318-323 omits it). The shape's defining behavior (the baton-pass leader animating to the ONE next lit part) cannot fire without it. Incomplete-state (words-only route, auto-light nothing) IS defined. But this means the fork shape's HERO mechanic (the directed leader to the next part) is undrawable until the loader delta ships ‚Äî and the loader delta is 'propose, do not apply.' So the fork shape ships as words-only for V2.
airGaps.fix: Honest degrade is defined, so not a blocker ‚Äî but the loader-select delta for routesToTestActionId should be elevated from 'propose' to a build prerequisite if the baton-pass is meant to be in V2, otherwise the fork shape's signature visual never appears.

constraintViolations.constraint: do-not-change-loader-contract / propose-do-not-apply: the plan correctly proposes loader deltas, BUT it then defines 7+ shapes whose HERO state (not just incomplete state) is unreachable without those deltas (EXPECT plate needs the 4 dropped columns; fork needs routesToTestActionId; trace needs meterMode). The brief says screens must render from CURRENT data. The plan blurs 'this is the V2 screen' with 'this is the screen AFTER Brandon approves loader changes.'
constraintViolations.where: Every electrical shape's EXPECT plate (claims expectedValue/expectedUnit/expectedTolerance as the hero digit) + fork baton-pass + all 4 trace shapes.
constraintViolations.fix: Split each shape's spec into 'renders today on current loader' vs 'renders after the proposed (approved) loader delta.' The reviewer found that on TODAY's loader, EVERY electrical EXPECT plate degrades to prose (no number), the fork is words-only, and traces are wireState-synthesized only. State this honestly so Brandon isn't shown a number-hero gauge that current data cannot produce.

constraintViolations.constraint: Premium-not-default-AI / data-driven zero-per-case: the duty-PWM and tier34 shapes assert the duty WINDOW (e.g. 45-75%) renders as a number, but VERIFIED the PWM readings in real data are pure prose ('PWM ¬∑ moderate duty', 'PWM ¬∑ MAX duty') with expectedValue NULL. The plan's own honest fallback (qualitative band, no fabricated %) is correct ‚Äî but the shape spec's primary description leads with the fabricated-looking numeric window, risking a build that invents 45-75% where data has only prose. constraintViolations.constraint: Provenance honesty / reconcile wording: the visual spec correctly catches that PROVENANCE_LABEL renders GAP as 'needs field verification' (VERIFIED at detail-panel line 66) while the standing chip says 'needs field check' (VERIFIED topology-node line 65). constraintViolations.constraint: Premium-not-default-AI (no verdict pills): visual-spec tokensToAdd is [] (empty), yet marks/wireVocab/gauge/provenance bodies depend on 9 tokens VERIFIED undefined in app: the six --role-* (referenced in topology.css lines 283-288 etc. but absent from globals.css), --vt-recede (referenced nowhere in topology.css ‚Äî prototype-only; plan's claim it's 'referenced across topology.css' is FALSE), and --vt-amber-600 (referenced topology.css lines 136/199/251 but globals.css defines only up to --vt-amber-500). With tokensToAdd empty, a builder following the visual spec literally adds zero tokens and every role-colored wire + recede + amber-ink renders broken/colorless.

dataModelHoles: meterMode as the sub-shape discriminator is VERIFIED unpopulated: the column exists only as DDL (migration 0021 line 110) and is NULL in all data; no seed/migration sets it. So the ENTIRE STEP-2 sub-shape selector (volts vs drop vs ohms vs duty vs amps) runs ONLY on the roleAbbreviation fallback today. That fallback maps '12V'‚Üívolts, 'GND'‚Üíohms, 'PWM'‚Üíduty ‚Äî which means voltage-DROP and AMPS/CLAMP have NO selector path on current data (no role abbreviation distinguishes a drop test from a plain voltage test, and no pin role says 'amps'). Voltage-drop and amps/clamp are therefore UN-SELECTABLE from current data ‚Äî they require curator-authored meterMode that no tooling provisions. This is per-case authoring masquerading as data-driven.

The EXPECT numeric band (expectedValue/expectedUnit/expectedTolerance) is VERIFIED NULL across the scene (columns are DDL-only, never populated). So the 'value-against-band gauge' that is the THESIS hero of every electrical+pressure+PID shape has no data to drive it on the seeded scene. Every gauge degrades to prose-band. The shape system's headline claim ('turns the prose-only Meter into a real EXPECT-vs-band gauge') is true only AFTER both a loader change AND a curator data-fill campaign that the plan does not schedule ‚Äî the screen cannot be driven from current data for its primary state.

isOutOfRange (the clean red/verdict handle) is a proposed column. Until it exists, the verdictAndRangeModel's Tier-2 is dormant, Tier-1 is dormant (no structured measured number is loaded ‚Äî tech_outcomes is unloaded and not in Drizzle defs, VERIFIED by its absence from the loader imports), leaving ONLY Tier-3 (branch_logic.verdict='fail' on a resolved fork). So for any NON-fork reading on current data, the verdict is permanently NEUTRAL ‚Äî the failure-tree-unfold (a core per-step-type behavior) can NEVER fire from a scenario flip alone. The 'flip to fault-low and the tree unfolds red' demo in multiple sceneExamples is undeliverable from current data.

Voltage-drop span requires component_connections.fromPinId/toPinId, VERIFIED 'mostly null' and unprovisioned. A shape whose hero needs a span that no row populates is a shape that secretly needs per-case authoring before it can render its non-degraded state.

The chassis-ground node for resistance endpoint B is not modeled (plan-admitted, scene has 25 components none of which is chassis-ground). The 'derive synthetic earth from a ground-role connection with null toPin' path depends on a connection row not confirmed to exist (only 5 electrical-wire connections total; ground-to-chassis edge unverified in the slice). The data cannot guarantee the second endpoint.
adversarial.verdict: air-gaps-found ‚Äî every named shape has a screen + an incomplete-state on paper, but coverage breaks on real data: 4 waveform shapes select on a trigger (waveform_capture) absent from all repo data; voltage-drop/amps/clamp/ripple/diode/min-max have no data-driven selector or no rendering primitive; and every EXPECT-band gauge + fork baton + verdict-unfold needs proposed-not-applied loader/columns, so the shapes ship in their degraded state, not their hero state.

adversarial.lens: Standing constraints: AI-word leaks, step-N-of-M / upcoming-work previews, mobile 375-414px gate, provenance honesty (no fabricated numbers), default-AI aesthetic, zero-per-case-design, and loader-contract-additive-only. Verified every load-bearing claim against the real loader, schema.ts, topology.css, globals.css, and scene-data.json.

airGaps.where: FRP sensor reference pins (frp-5v role '5V', frp-lref role 'LREF') ‚Äî VERIFIED in scene-data.json pins list. NO shape design covers them and the shapeSelector roleAbbreviation fallback table only lists '12V'/'5V'/'SIG'‚Üívolts and 'GND'‚Üíohms and 'PWM'‚Üíduty. 'LREF' (low-reference) maps to NOTHING in any selector branch, and '5V' is listed but no shape design (voltage-DC, continuity, drop, duty, waveform) names the 5V-ref or low-ref test as a covered case. When meterMode is null (today's reality) and the tech taps frp-5v or frp-lref, the tool does not know what to draw.
airGaps.fix: Add explicit selector rows: roleAbbreviation '5V'‚Üívolts value-plate (EXPECT ~5V ref), 'LREF'‚Üívolts value-plate (EXPECT ~0V/low-ref, or continuity-to-ground). Map 'LREF' in the shapeSelector convention alongside 'GND'. The voltage-DC shape already covers the screen; the gap is purely that the selector convention omits 'LREF' and never names 5V-ref/low-ref as instances, so they silently fall to 'default volts value-plate' which is correct for 5V but wrong-framed for LREF (a low-ref should EXPECT ~0V, not a 5V supply). State the LREF‚Üílow-ref mapping explicitly.

airGaps.where: scenarioRequired = 'none' ‚Äî VERIFIED as a real enum value (schema.ts:871). LOCATE and look/inspect shapes claim the scenario is 'none'/'key-off' and say the chip 'dims or reads any state', but there is no defined NOW-column / scenario-chip render for scenarioRequired='none'. The scenarioChrome spec only defines labels derived from (keyPosition,engineState,loadLevel) for operation rows and slug for fault rows. A test_action with scenarioRequired='none' has no scenario row to fold into 'NOW ¬∑ ‚Ä¶' and no defined chip text.
airGaps.fix: Define the 'none' case: when test_actions.scenarioRequired='none', suppress the scenario chip entirely (no '‚ñæ', no 'any' hint) and the NOW column header reads just 'NOW' or, for LOCATE/look, nothing. Add this to scenarioChrome as an explicit third branch alongside operation-tuple and fault-slug.

airGaps.where: Two observationMethod columns both loaded ‚Äî VERIFIED: observableProperties.observationMethod (loader line 260) AND test_actions.observationMethod (loader line 303). EVERY shape design says 'shape = test_actions.observationMethod' but the loader surfaces TopologyComponent.observableProperties[] each carrying its OWN observationMethod. The resolver does not say which one wins when a component has an observableProperty with observationMethod='scan_tool_pid' but the focused test_action has observationMethod='electrical_measurement_at_pin'. The tool would not know which method picks the shape.
airGaps.fix: State explicitly in shapeSelector: the shape is selected from the CURRENT STEP's test_action.observationMethod ONLY; observableProperties.observationMethod is never a shape selector (it describes a property, not the active step). Add one sentence ruling out the observableProperties column.

airGaps.where: PWM shape selection is triply contradictory across designs ‚Äî same pin, three different shapes. (1) data-model shapeSelector + circuit-path list duty under observationMethod='electrical_measurement_at_pin' (meterMode 'duty'). (2) electrical/duty-pwm triggers ONLY on observationMethod='waveform_capture' + roleAbbreviation='PWM'. (3) electrical.waveform.scope ALSO triggers on 'waveform_capture' + 'PWM'. (4) electrical-tier34-graceful routes BOTH 'electrical_measurement_at_pin' AND 'waveform_capture' to duty. For the scene's vcv-a/reg-a (roleAbbreviation 'PWM'), the actual test_action observationMethod is unverifiable from the slice (test_actions not in scene-data.json), so the tool cannot deterministically pick duty-pwm vs waveform.scope vs tier34 ‚Äî they overlap on identical inputs.
airGaps.fix: Collapse the overlap. Define ONE precedence: observationMethod='waveform_capture' ALWAYS ‚Üí scope/trace shape (electrical.waveform.scope), and within it roleAbbreviation 'PWM'‚Üísquare trace, 'SIG'‚Üíanalog. observationMethod='electrical_measurement_at_pin' + meterMode='duty' (or roleAbbreviation 'PWM' fallback) ‚Üí the duty RING gauge (electrical/duty-pwm), NOT a trace. Delete duty-pwm's 'waveform_capture' trigger (it conflicts with waveform.scope) OR delete waveform.scope's PWM branch ‚Äî they cannot both own 'waveform_capture'+'PWM'. Make electrical-tier34-graceful explicitly the SAME canvas as the basic shapes (it claims this) and remove its independent selector so it never competes.

airGaps.where: voltage-DROP across-two-points span endpoints ‚Äî the across-two hookup needs component_connections.fromPinId/toPinId (hookupModel + meterHookupOverlay rely on these). VERIFIED in CRITICAL FACTS these are 'loaded, currently mostly null'. For the canonical drop case (pump-GND ‚Üí chassis stud) the chassis stud is NOT a modeled component (only 25 components, none is chassis-ground per the look/inspect+continuity shapes' own admission). So the span has no second endpoint AND fromPinId/toPinId are null ‚Üí the across-two bracket overlay has nowhere to draw the second lead. The incomplete-state ('one probe mark + description + amber note') is defined, but the SUCCESS state is an air gap: with the proposed authoring there is still no chassis-ground NODE to anchor lead B.
airGaps.fix: Either (a) require a chassis-ground reference component in the seed (a real components row, kind='mechanical'/'connector') so fromPinId/toPinId can both resolve to nodes, or (b) define the synthetic earth-glyph endpoint as the FIRST-CLASS success render (not just incomplete fallback) for any ground-role connection with toPinId=null. The continuity shape already proposes the synthetic earth glyph; make voltage-drop reuse it rather than treating a null second endpoint as degraded.

airGaps.where: ORIENT step shape has no worked screen. stepKind enum includes 'orient' (newFieldsProposed) and locate.shapeName is 'Locate / Orient', but every concrete description, dataField, meterBlock, and sceneExample is LOCATE (find the part, location string hero). ORIENT is described only as 'names the circuit, elevates no pin, shows no reading' ‚Äî there is no defined hero element, no defined Meter content, no defined canvas framing for ORIENT. A tool given stepKind='orient' would not know what to draw in the Meter band (LOCATE leads with a location string; ORIENT has explicitly no pin and no location-of-a-part).
airGaps.fix: Give ORIENT its own defined screen: Meter leads with the SYSTEM name + the circuit's plain-English purpose (from components.function / the symptom's system), canvas frames the named circuit at uniform focus with NO active pin and NO leader, no EXPECT/NOW. Define its honest incomplete-state (system name missing ‚Üí show symptom.system slug). Today it is an unscreened enum value.

airGaps.where: stepKind 'interpret' is a proposed enum value ('locate'|'orient'|'probe'|'interpret'|'branch') with NO shape design at all. 'probe' and 'branch' map to existing shapes, 'locate'/'orient' are (partly) covered, but 'interpret' appears nowhere in the 13 PER-SHAPE designs. A test_action authored with stepKind='interpret' has no defined screen.
airGaps.fix: Either drop 'interpret' from the proposed enum (it is unused ‚Äî keep the enum minimal per Rule 2), or define its screen. Recommend dropping it; the fork/decision shape already covers reading-interpretation.

airGaps.where: The 'NOW ¬∑ Key On ¬∑ Engine Off' canonical label is used as the hero scenario framing in circuit-path, voltage-DC, and confirm-complaint, but VERIFIED the scene's 'key-on' scenario has engineState='off' and the DEFAULT scenario is 'idle' (is_default=true), NOT key-on. On session entry the active scenario will be idle, so the entry/voltage screens will actually render 'NOW ¬∑ Engine Idle', not the 'Key On ¬∑ Engine Off' shown throughout the designs. Not a fabrication, but the worked examples assume a non-default starting scenario with no defined rule for WHICH scenario is active at step entry.
airGaps.fix: Define the active-scenario-at-entry rule: default to system_scenarios.isDefault (=idle here), OR to the test_action's scenarioRequired when set. The voltage-DC canonical reading at idle is '12V steady' (verified in scene readings), so the worked 'briefly during prime then 0V' (key-on) example only appears if scenarioRequired forces key-on. State that scenarioRequired overrides isDefault for the step's NOW.

airGaps.where: amps/clamp and Hz and AC-ripple and diode and min-max sub-modes (electrical-tier34-graceful) have NO instance in the scene and the design admits it ('AC ripple and diode test have NO component in this scene'). That is acceptable per the brief IF a defined honest incomplete-state exists ‚Äî and one does (degrade to name+hookup+'EXPECT ‚Äî needs field check', suppress trace). BUT the amps/clamp gauge for the lift-pump current draw is presented as a canonical scene instance ('Lift Pump 12V pin under an amps/clamp test') while VERIFIED the lift pump's electricalContract says 'current draw ‚Ä¶ not yet captured' and meterMode is null ‚Äî so the amps shape would be SELECTED (if a curator authors meterMode='amps') but has no expectedValue, leaving the EXPECT plate to honestly degrade. The gap: the amps clamp overlay needs the wire to clamp around, but a single-pin test_action (componentId only) does not say WHICH connection edge to encircle when a component has multiple wires (lp-12v feed vs lp-gnd return).
airGaps.fix: Define which edge the clamp ring encircles: the connection whose fromPinId/toPinId matches the focused pin, else the connection whose electricalRole matches the pin's roleAbbreviation ('12V'‚ÜíelectricalRole '12v'). Add this disambiguation to meterHookupOverlay's CLAMP branch.

constraintViolations.constraint: Provenance honesty / no-fabricated-number ‚Äî wording self-conflict that risks dishonest provenance display
constraintViolations.where: provenance spec says reconcile GAP wording: caption uses PROVENANCE_LABEL verbatim ('needs field verification') and reserves 'needs field check' for the inline m-fact eyebrow. VERIFIED the shipped code at topology-node.tsx:65 renders the standing chip text 'needs field check' and PROVENANCE_LABEL (topology-detail-panel.tsx:66) maps GAP‚Üí'needs field verification'. The plan reuses BOTH strings. But the scene has ZERO GAP components (all 25 + 9 pins TRAINING-CONFIRMED, VERIFIED). So 'needs field verification' (the GAP caption) will NEVER fire in this scene, while 'needs field check' (the amber labelGap register) fires constantly. The two-phrasing reconciliation is fine, but the plan repeatedly attributes the amber register to provenance ‚Äî it is NOT provenance, it is labelGap/electricalContract content. Conflating them risks the amber 'needs field check' reading as a provenance grade the data does not assign.
constraintViolations.fix: State unambiguously: the amber 'needs field check' register is sourced from labelGap/electricalContract ONLY and is NEVER a provenance grade. Provenance grade for this entire scene is uniformly 'from theory' (graphite). Keep the GAP‚Üí'needs field verification' caption code-matched but note it is dormant in this scene.

constraintViolations.constraint: do-not-change-loader-contract (additive only) ‚Äî one delta is NOT purely additive
constraintViolations.where: loaderContractDelta proposes surfacing isOutOfRange onto TopologyScenario as a NEW parallel map 'pinOutOfRange: Record<pinId, boolean>'. That is additive. BUT the verdictAndRangeModel Tier-1 path computes in-range by comparing a MEASURED number that 'tech_outcomes.measured_value ‚Ä¶ is NOT loaded'. To make Tier-1 live, the loader would have to load tech_outcomes ‚Äî VERIFIED tech_outcomes is NOT in Drizzle defs (only in migration 0021 SQL). Wiring a new table into the loader is a larger change than 'un-dropping columns', and the plan elsewhere frames Tier-1 as near-shippable. This under-states the loader work and the Drizzle-defs gap.
constraintViolations.fix: Mark Tier-1 (numeric verdict) as explicitly DEFERRED: it requires (a) adding tech_outcomes to Drizzle defs and (b) a new loader join ‚Äî neither is a column-un-drop. Ship on Tier-2 (isOutOfRange boolean) + Tier-3 (branch verdict) + NEUTRAL default only. This keeps the contract change purely additive.

constraintViolations.constraint: mobile 375-414px hard gate ‚Äî clamp-ring legibility unproven at the gate
constraintViolations.where: amps/clamp overlay mobile spec says 'the clamp-ring glyph around the pin-A wire renders at ‚â•20px so the induction-not-touch read survives small'. The mobile constraint requires ‚â•44/48px TAP targets; a 20px clamp ring is a VISUAL glyph not a tap target, but the across-two bracket arc and the gauge-tee glyph have no stated minimum size at 375px, and the waveform trace window at peek (~56px tall) plus a graticule may not resolve a reference-vs-live square wave at 375px width. No acceptance gate is defined for trace legibility at 375px (only for the Turo camera-pan).
constraintViolations.fix: Add a 375px legibility acceptance gate for: (1) the dual-trace scope window (reference ghost distinguishable from live ink), (2) the across-two bracket arc, (3) the gauge-tee glyph ‚Äî each must be visually resolvable, tested at 375px, the same way the camera-pan is gated.

dataModelHoles: Verdict for OPERATION scenarios (non-fault) cannot be driven from data without per-case authoring. Tier-1 (numeric) is dormant (no loaded measured number), Tier-2 isOutOfRange is described as 'set true ONLY for fault-scenario x symptom-pin pairs' ‚Äî so operation-scenario readings ('12V steady' at idle) have NO structured verdict source and default to NEUTRAL forever. That means the in-range '‚óê in range' navy chip shown in nearly every worked example CANNOT actually render from data for operation scenarios; it would require a curator to author isOutOfRange=false per (pin,operation-scenario), which is per-case authoring. The 'at-target' green/navy state is effectively unreachable data-drivenly.

The confirm-complaint RED-at-entry verdict depends on isOutOfRange being authored for the (symptom-pin √ó fault scenario) pair, but the entry screen's active scenario at session start is isDefault=idle (an OPERATION scenario), not fault-low. So at entry the FRP signal pin's reading is the idle operation reading, which has no isOutOfRange flag ‚Üí NEUTRAL, not RED. The 'complaint already failed, red lit at entry' hero requires the entry screen to force the fault scenario, which is a per-symptom authoring decision with no defined data source (symptom‚Üífault-scenario link does not exist in the schema).

voltage-drop as a DISTINCT shape requires component_connections.fromPinId/toPinId to be authored to express the span; the plan admits these are 'mostly null' today. Until authored per-connection, every voltage-drop test_action is INDISTINGUISHABLE from a single-point read (same observationMethod, same component) and falls back to one-lead-to-ground ‚Äî i.e. the shape silently does not appear. This is a per-case authoring requirement masquerading as data-driven, for the one shape the brief calls the master-tech differentiator.

The reference WAVEFORM shape (the fixed known-good trace the scope overlays against) has no data source ‚Äî the plan synthesizes it from wireState enum. wireState is loaded, so the synthesized trace IS data-driven, but the VERDICT 'matches reference / wrong shape' compares live-wireState against expected-wireState with no stored expected-wireState per (pin,scenario) other than the live one. There is no 'known-good wireState' column distinct from the scenario's actual wireState, so the scope verdict cannot data-drivenly say 'this trace is wrong' without either isOutOfRange or scenario.kind='fault' ‚Äî same hole as the numeric verdict.

symptom‚Üíhero-pin link (confirm-complaint) is proposed as derivable from 'highest symptom_test_implications.priority test_action's componentId/pin'. But a test_action has a componentId, not a pinId ‚Äî a component can have multiple pins (FRP sensor has frp-5v/frp-lref/frp-signal). Deriving the HERO PIN (frp-signal specifically) from a test_action that only names the component is ambiguous; picking the right pin requires either the new symptoms.symptomPinId column or per-case heuristic. The 'free derivation' claim has a pin-resolution hole.
adversarial.verdict: air-gaps-found ‚Äî 10 air gaps (2 blocker: uncovered 5V-ref/LREF reference pins and triple-overlapping PWM shape selection; 5 major), 3 constraint violations, and 5 data-model holes where operation-scenario verdicts, voltage-drop spans, and the entry red-state secretly require per-case authoring.

adversarial.lens: Data-drivability: can each shape's screen render automatically from the proposed model + real columns with zero per-case authoring? I verified every load-bearing claim against lib/diagnostics/load-system-topology.ts, lib/db/schema.ts (lines 860-949, 1083-1113), .design-shots/scene-data.json, app/globals.css, components/topology/topology.css, topology-node.tsx, topology-detail-panel.tsx, and .design-shots/mockups/proto-meter.html. The shape-selection spine (observationMethod loaded; meterMode/expectedValue/expectedUnit/expectedTolerance/routesToTestActionId/reasoning exist but dropped by the loader SELECT) is ALL true. The verdict/range honesty model (prose-only readings, NEUTRAL-when-ambiguous) is sound and correctly self-aware. The fatal class of air gap is the GROUND REFERENCE: it is treated as a token/CSS gap when it is actually a missing data primitive that breaks the canvas of multiple shapes.

airGaps.where: PARENT circuit-path + electrical/voltage-dc + locate shapes ‚Äî the GROUND LEG / ground reference / chassis-ground node. All three assert the canvas draws 'source -> part -> ground' with a graphite --role-ground wire from the lift pump down to Chassis Ground, and voltage-DC draws 'a faint reference lead-dot on the ground symbol'. VERIFIED IN scene-data.json: there is NO connection with electricalRole='ground' (roles present are only pwm/low-ref/5v-ref/12v/signal); the lp-gnd pin has ZERO connections; the Electric Lift Pump's only electrical-wire is the incoming 12v; and there is NO chassis-ground component among the 25. The ground half of every one-lead-to-ground hookup, the second lead-dot, and the locate ground edge have no data to render from.
airGaps.fix: Reclassify the ground reference from a CSS-token gap to a DATA-MODEL primitive. Either (a) seed a chassis-ground component (kind='connector'/'mechanical') + an electrical-wire connection electricalRole='ground' from each grounded part to it with fromPin/toPin set, OR (b) define a render rule: when a part has a GND-role pin with no ground connection, draw a synthetic earth glyph at that pin's edge (the continuity shape already proposes this ‚Äî promote that rule to the PARENT circuit-path so volts-DC, continuity, and locate all share it). Until one ships, the one-lead-to-ground overlay, the across-A/B continuity span, and the locate ground edge are undrawable and must honestly suppress the ground mark, not draw a phantom line.

airGaps.where: electrical/duty-pwm, waveform.scope, current-clamp-scope, tier34-graceful, and the 'cross-check pin B lights as the next move' behavior ‚Äî the complementary B pins. airGaps.where: confirm-complaint sceneExample ‚Äî the NOW self-label 'NOW ¬∑ Engine Cranking'. airGaps.where: electrical-voltage-drop hookup overlay ('TWO bright lead-dots, one at each end of the SAME conductor segment') and the across-two endpoints. constraintViolations.constraint: provenance-honesty / do-not-change-loader-contract (the six --role-* + --vt-recede + --vt-amber-600 tokens are scoped as a trivial 'one-line :root fix', understating that the wires render colorless TODAY in the shipped app). dataModelHoles: GROUND REFERENCE IS A MISSING PRIMITIVE, NOT A TOKEN GAP: zero connections have electricalRole='ground', the lp-gnd pin has zero connections, and there is no chassis-ground node. Every shape that draws 'to ground' (parent circuit-path, voltage-DC one-lead-to-ground, continuity A<->B span, locate pump->chassis edge) is secretly authoring the ground end per-case in the prototype's HTML because the data cannot supply it. The fix (synthetic earth glyph rule OR seeded ground node+connection) must be a SHARED render primitive, or each shape silently needs hand-placed ground marks.

COMPLEMENTARY B-PIN WIRING ABSENT: vcv-b/reg-b have pins but no connections, so any shape that draws a wire/trace/leader to B (two-pin compare, cross-check-pin-B baton-pass) needs per-case authoring of the B edge ‚Äî it cannot come from data. Only the pin CHIP is data-driven; the WIRE to it is not.

NO DUAL-PINNED CONNECTION EXISTS: the voltage-drop across-two-points span requires a connection with BOTH fromPinId and toPinId; in the real scene no connection has both set. So the across-two overlay (bracket spanning a conductor) cannot be composed from data for any current case ‚Äî it is a per-case authoring requirement masquerading as a derived overlay.

FAULT-SCENARIO TUPLE IS NULL: fault scenarios carry no (keyPosition,engineState,loadLevel), so any NOW self-label derived from the tuple returns blank for the two fault scenarios that confirm-complaint and the fault-tree reveals depend on. The label must come from scenario.label/slug for faults ‚Äî drivable, but a different code path than operation scenarios, and the plan's examples conflate them.

VERDICT RED FOR OPERATION SCENARIOS HAS NO STRUCTURED SOURCE: with tech_outcomes unloaded and readings prose-only, the in-range/out-of-range judgment for the six operation scenarios cannot be computed from data ‚Äî only fault scenarios (via scenario.kind or the proposed isOutOfRange) can ever go red. Any operation-scenario gauge that implies a measured verdict is authoring that verdict per-case; the honest state is permanent NEUTRAL on operation scenarios until a measured-value table is loaded.
adversarial.verdict: air-gaps-found ‚Äî the shape-selection spine and verdict-honesty model are sound and data-verified, but the ground reference (and B-pin / across-two-span wiring) is a missing data primitive treated as a CSS gap, leaving the ground/span hookup of four shapes undrawable from data without per-case authoring.

electrical PROBE ‚Äî lift pump 12V (one-lead-to-ground hookup, warm-red animated 12V flow, EXPECT 12V hero, leader to Meter)

build.changelog: Reordered + extended the walk from 3 electrical steps to 6 shapes: confirm-complaint -> probe-12v -> locate-ground -> single-value PID -> look/inspect -> fork. KEPT the original electrical PROBE + LOCATE working (probe-12v, locate-ground) and re-cast them inside a generalized shape resolver (STEPS[].shape = confirm|probe|locate|single-value|look|fork) so each step REDRAWS the canvas + Meter to exactly its shape. Deleted the old .topo-node faceplate fill (.node .plate background now transparent) so a mark is a role-colored POINT + serif name only. Replaced the 1.4px border vocabulary with the point+ring marks. Added the role-colored point system per component kind/electrical role via data-pt. Added 3 new Meter modes (good-vs-bad COMPARE for look, VERDICT->ROUTE for fork, prose-band EXPECT for confirm/PID) plus a gated failure-tree unfold and the look-verdict tap target. Added the derived meter-hookup overlay (#hookup SVG: one-lead-to-ground probe glyph + faint reference lead). Added a baton-pass route leader (.wire.baton draw-on, frozen under reduced-motion). DEFINED all 9 previously-undefined tokens the adversarial review flagged: the six --role-* with the exact topology.css names (incl. --role-pwm violet, --role-low-ref, --role-5v-ref), --vt-recede:0.16, --vt-amber-600. Switched the amber provenance/labelGap ink from --vt-risk-high to --vt-amber-600. Added FRP 5V + LREF pins (pwm/lref/5v dot roles). Added scenarioRequired handling: a step's lock overrides the active scenario; 'none' dims the chip to 'any key state' + NOW reads just 'Now'; free steps fall back to the default (idle) at entry instead of inheriting a prior lock. Switched .wire.off from display:none to the existing fade-not-vanish behavior is preserved via whole-system. Fixed a dead double-negative in the look-fault handler.
build.selfReview: SOLID: All 6 shapes render distinctly and were screenshot-verified at 1320px desktop AND 375px mobile; zero application JS errors across a full forward+back walk (only a harmless favicon 404). The per-step-TYPE fact budget thesis is visibly proven ‚Äî LOCATE suppresses the reading entirely (no EXPECT/NOW/verdict), single-value PID drops the probe leads, look replaces the gauge with a compare, fork drops the EXPECT band. Three-color discipline is honest: confirm-complaint and PID ship NEUTRAL graphite (not red) because isOutOfRange/symptomPinId are unbuilt columns ‚Äî never coloring a reading wrong we cannot prove wrong; red appears ONLY when the human taps 'fault seen' on the look step. No 'AI' word, no 'step N of M', no rectangles/cards (marks are points + serif names). Mobile pan-coupling acceptance gate passes: on the probe step at 375px the lit pump-under-test sits above the bottom sheet, not under it. All flagged undefined tokens are now defined with the exact app names. ROUGH: the meter-hookup overlay is implemented for the one-lead-to-ground electrical case only (the canonical case in this walk); across-two bracket / clamp-ring / scope-lead / gauge-tee glyphs are NOT drawn because no step in this 6-shape walk needs them (no vdrop/amps/waveform/pressure step is in scope) ‚Äî they would be the next shapes to add, not regressions. The leader-line math uses getBoundingClientRect screen->world conversion which is correct but is recomputed on a rAF rather than continuously, so a mid-animation camera settle can momentarily lag the leader by one frame (cosmetic, settles correctly). The fork's 'slide toward center' is a fixed -46px nudge rather than a computed midpoint ‚Äî fine for this scene, would want to be data-driven for arbitrary geometry. NOT rectangles anywhere; the only framed boxes are the EXPECT plate (an instrument readout, intentional) and the look-compare swatches (hairline-framed per spec) and the Meter card itself ‚Äî all sanctioned by the spec, none are part faceplates.
knownRisks: The meter-hookup overlay only draws the one-lead-to-ground electrical form (the case this walk exercises). across-two bracket, amps clamp-ring, scope lead, and fluid gauge-tee glyphs are specified but NOT implemented here because no step in this 6-shape walk triggers them ‚Äî they are future shapes, but a reviewer expecting all overlay forms in this file will not find them.

Per the data model + adversarial review, the red-at-entry confirm-complaint and any numeric verdict are deliberately shipped as honest NEUTRAL degrades ‚Äî isOutOfRange, symptomPinId, the four EXPECT columns, routesToTestActionId, and stepKind are all proposed/unbuilt, so the prototype demonstrates the SHAPES with hardcoded demo data, not a live data-driven verdict. This is intentional and matches the brief, but means the gauge needle never settles against a real captured number.

The leader line and hookup glyphs are positioned by approximating the active pin at a fixed offset below the active node mark (node.x-10, node.y+30) rather than reading the actual pin chip's transformed position into world space; it lines up well for the scene's geometry but is not a general pin-anchoring solution.

Reduced-motion was verified via source inspection + the fact that the route wire renders at full 3.5px with the baton animation frozen to offset 0; it was not screenshot-captured under an emulated prefers-reduced-motion media state, so the visual-identical claim rests on the CSS guard being correct rather than a captured frame.

The fork 'slide toward center' uses a fixed -46px nudge to the next node's left position; it is reset correctly on step change but is geometry-specific to this scene rather than computed from the cleared run's midpoint.
